Please send your portfolio + application and make sure the job title includes:



???? “US-Based Company – Figma/UI-UX Designer”



Remote position. Long-term opportunity.

title: Offer Architect / Direct Response Expert
description:
We are a global media company with a fast-growing Direct Response division. We create offers across Nutra, Dating, Survival, BizOpp, Finance, and Personal Development. Our work touches platforms like ClickBank, Digistore, Meta, YouTube, Native, and TikTok. We already have an amazing team in Manila and are now expanding.



We are hiring a REMOTE PH-based Offer Architect / Direct Response Copywriter, available part-time or full-time. This role is for someone who understands real DR fundamentals—offer architecture, VSL storytelling, big ideas, unique mechanisms, emotional triggers, and high-converting funnel structures—not traditional branding.



You will help shape complete offers from start to finish: big idea ? mechanism ? angles ? hooks ? VSL structure ? presells ? upsells. If you have experience in any direct response vertical, you’re welcome to apply.



Your responsibilities include:



• Creating offer architectures and mechanism-based positioning

• Writing long-form VSLs (8k–20k words) in clear Grade-5 English

• Building presell pages, advertorials, quiz funnels, and upsell paths

• Developing hook libraries, angle matrices, and curiosity-driven opens

• Collaborating with media buyers to optimize CPA, AOV, EPC, and funnel flow

• Studying competitor funnels and reverse engineering winners

• Creating compliant cold-traffic messaging for Meta & YouTube

• Using DR frameworks (RMBC, Agora principles, Todd Brown USP)

• Working with AI tools for research, ideation, and B-roll briefing



We offer long-term stability, weekly pay, and the chance to grow inside a global media ecosystem with a strong presence in Manila. You’ll join a supportive performance team that values speed, clarity, creativity, and execution.



If you have real DR experience and want to work on ambitious, high-impact offers, we would love to meet you.

title: Project Manager (Venture Studio Level)
description:
Project Manager / Product Manager





We're a multi-product venture studio moving at startup speed. We launch, test, and scale new businesses in weeks. To keep the machine running, we need a Strategic Project Manager who not only thrives on systems and automation but also values autonomy in their work. Without a strong PM, fast-paced ventures collapse into chaos. You will transform raw ideas into roadmaps, build automated workflows, and ensure execution across global teams.





Responsibilities:

1. Translate the founder's vision into structured roadmaps with milestones and accountability.

2. Build automated workflows (Zapier, Make, n8n) to eliminate bottlenecks.

title: Tiktok Shop  Manager
description:
ABOUT INSPIRATEK

Inspiratek LLC is a US-based e-commerce company generating $18M+ annually on Amazon, with expanding presence on TikTok Shop and DTC Shopify. We operate 5 private label brands with 20+ active products across the portfolio.

What makes us different: we're building one of the most data-driven operations in the private label space. Our team uses advanced analytics, AI-powered operational agents, custom data pipelines, and structured decision frameworks to manage every aspect of the business , from keyword-level PPC decisions to inventory forecasting to competitive intelligence. This isn't a company where you'll be making gut calls. Every decision is backed by data, and every action plan has measurable outcomes.

We're a 27-person distributed team across the world. We operate in a fast-paced, high-autonomy environment where ownership and accountability matter more than hours logged.



THE ROLE

You will be the single owner of TikTok Shop operations across our entire brand portfolio. This is not a content creation role or a virtual assistant position — this is a channel management role with full P&L responsibility. You will manage the affiliate/creator ecosystem, oversee GMV Max ad campaigns, coordinate product launches on TikTok, and drive profitable revenue growth.

You report directly to the CEO. You will work cross-functionally with our Amazon operations team, creative/product development team, logistics team, and external agencies. You will be evaluated on revenue growth, profitability, creator activation rates, and content velocity — not activity metrics.



WHAT YOU WILL OWN

TikTok Shop Strategy & Revenue Growth

Develop and execute the TikTok Shop strategy across all brands (currently 5 bedding brands)

Own the channel P&L — you are responsible for GMV, ROAS, net margin, and CAC

Set monthly/quarterly revenue targets and build the execution plan to hit them

Identify and capitalize on TikTok trends, seasonal opportunities, and promotional events (e.g., Super Brand Day, Mega Sales)

Manage product catalog on TikTok Seller Center — listings, pricing, inventory coordination, promotions

Coordinate with Amazon ops to prevent cross-channel cannibalization and align launch timing

Creator & Affiliate Program Management

Build and scale a high-performing creator network (target: 200+ active creators within 6 months)

Recruit creators through Affiliate Center, manual outreach, and community building

Develop tiered commission structures that reward performance without destroying margin

Manage product sampling/seeding program — budget allocation, creator selection criteria, fulfillment coordination

Track creator activation rates (% of seeded creators who actually post) and implement re-engagement sequences

Build a creator community (Slack/
) with content calendars, best practices, and incentive programs

Coach creators on content optimization: hooks, CTAs, product demonstration, FTC compliance

GMV Max & Paid Advertising

Manage GMV Max campaigns with budgets of $500–$5,000+/day across multiple brands

Structure campaigns by product, objective, and creative type

Optimize for ROAS, not just GMV — every dollar spent must be justified by margin contribution

Identify and amplify top-performing organic creator content via Spark Ads

A/B test creative angles, hooks, and CTAs at scale

Know when to scale, when to pause, and when to kill — with data to support every decision

Performance Analytics & Reporting

Build and maintain weekly performance reports covering: GMV, orders, AOV, ROAS, CAC, creator posting frequency, content velocity, affiliate commission costs, net margin

Identify trends before they become problems — declining CVR, creator churn, rising CPA, content velocity drops

Calculate true profitability per product on TikTok (revenue minus COGS, commissions, ad spend, sampling costs, fulfillment, returns)

Present data-driven recommendations to leadership with clear trade-offs and financial impact

Cross-Functional Coordination

• Align TikTok launches with Amazon product launches and inventory planning

• Work with creative team on product photography, video assets, and listing content optimized for TikTok

• Coordinate with logistics on TikTok-specific fulfillment (FBT, seller shipping, sample shipments)

• Manage relationships with external agencies or freelancers supporting TikTok operations



REQUIREMENTS

Must Have (Non-Negotiable)

2–4+ years of direct TikTok Shop management experience as a seller (not just as a creator or agency)

Proven track record of managing $50K+/month in TikTok Shop GMV with documented results

Hands-on experience with TikTok Seller Center, Affiliate Center, and GMV Max campaign management

Demonstrated ability to recruit, manage, and scale a creator/affiliate network of 50+ active creators

Strong analytical skills — comfortable building reports, calculating margins, and making data-driven decisions

Experience managing GMV Max campaigns with budgets of $500+/day and achieving ROAS > 3.0x

Financial literacy — understand unit economics, contribution margin, break-even ROAS

Native or near-native English proficiency

Strong Preference

Experience in home goods, bedding, lifestyle, or wellness categories on TikTok Shop

Experience managing multiple brands on TikTok simultaneously

Familiarity with TikTok Shop analytics tools (Kalodata, FastMoss, or similar)

Experience with product seeding/sampling programs at scale (100+ samples/month)

Understanding of Amazon operations (helpful for cross-channel coordination)

Experience with Live Shopping on TikTok

Nice to Have

Experience with TikTok Shop API integrations

Content creation skills (CapCut, video editing)

Experience scaling a TikTok Shop channel from

title: Amazon Logistics & Supply Chain Lead
description:
ABOUT US

Inspiratek LLC is a US-based e-commerce company generating $18M+ annually on Amazon, with expanding presence on TikTok Shop and DTC Shopify. We operate 5 private label brands with 20+ active products across the portfolio.

What makes us different: we're building one of the most data-driven operations in the private label space. Our team uses advanced analytics, AI-powered operational agents, custom data pipelines, and structured decision frameworks to manage every aspect of the business , from keyword-level PPC decisions to inventory forecasting to competitive intelligence. This isn't a company where you'll be making gut calls. Every decision is backed by data, and every action plan has measurable outcomes.

We're a 27-person distributed team across the world. We operate in a fast-paced, high-autonomy environment where ownership and accountability matter more than hours logged.



THE ROLE

This is one of our most critical hires. Our logistics department has more work than bandwidth, and we've learned through hard experience what this role needs. Two of our four strategic pillars — Supply Chain & Supplier Management and 3PL & FBA Operations — have minimal formal coverage today.

Here's the reality:

$30K+/month in Amazon storage fees, with LTSF as a compounding financial crisis needing systematic management

Supplier negotiation, freight optimization, and cost benchmarking remain unaddressed

Forecasting needs to mature from reactive to cross-functional — incorporating PPC, deals, channel data, and seasonal patterns

3PL strategy is nascent — needs formalization as a resilience layer

All China-based suppliers need consolidated relationship management

Without this hire, financial exposure continues to compound, lead times remain un-optimized, and the department cannot operate across all four pillars simultaneously.



WHAT YOU’LL OWN

Pillar 1: Inventory Intelligence & Demand Planning

Own days-of-supply (DOS) targets, safety stock methodology, and the forecasting process across all SKU tiers

Keep hero SKUs (60-80% of revenue) within target supply windows

Build cross-functional forecasting incorporating PPC spend, deals calendar, seasonal patterns, and channel-specific data

Own sell-through rate optimization across the portfolio

Pillar 2: Supply Chain & Supplier Management

Build this function from the ground up — your greenfield opportunity

Manage relationships with China-based suppliers across satin, bamboo, and cooling product lines

Establish the full PO lifecycle: issuance to production tracking to QC inspection to freight to receiving

Create supplier scorecards tracking lead time, defect rates, cost per unit, and responsiveness

Drive freight optimization and own COGS tracking end-to-end

Pillar 3: 3PL & FBA Operations

Define and track 3PL SLAs: processing time, storage costs, outbound accuracy, prep quality

Plan inbound shipments strategically to minimize placement fees

Build a reimbursement recovery process

Develop 3PL as a resilience buffer for demand spikes and supply chain disruption

Pillar 4: Cost Optimization & Storage Governance

Drive systematic fee mitigation across LTSF, SUR, shipping rates, and landed cost

Own the financial exposure dashboard

Build removal/liquidation ROI models

Make sure we NEVER get surprised by a cost cliff we should have seen coming

Pillar 5: Team Development & Process Formalization

Lead existing operators and grow the function

Document SOPs for ALL recurring processes

Establish cadences that run without prompting

Build a department that works even when you're not in the room



WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

First 30 Days

Complete onboarding across all four pillars — map current state, tools, team, pain points

Present a 90-day action plan with prioritized initiatives and quick wins

Establish working relationships with each tea
mber

First 90 Days

Independently running weekly KPI cadence — CEO not required in the room

Supply Chain and 3PL pillars documented with first improvements underway

Storage fee exposure under systematic management

First supplier scorecard completed; freight optimization opportunities identified

By 6 Months

All four pillars staffed and operating with documented SOPs

Cross-functional forecasting process live

LTSF fees trending down month-over-month

By 12 Months

KPIs trending positively: DOS, storage fees, forecast accuracy, landed cost

Department absorbs demand fluctuations without crisis escalation

Leadership oversight shifted to monthly strategic reviews



WHAT WERE LOOKING FOR

Requirements:

Strong proficiency in written and spoken English

4-6+ years in logistics or supply chain roles, 2+ years owning outcomes in a leadership or senior IC capacity

Hands-on Amazon FBA experience — LTSF mechanics, SUR, inbound planning, placement fees, fee structures

Experience managing at least one 3PL relationship end-to-end

Track record of building processes from scratch in a resource-constrained environment

Experience managing or building a small logistics team (3-8 people)

Advanced Excel / Google Sheets fluency — you'll build models, dashboards, and trackers from day one

Financial acuity — you translate logistics decisions into dollar impact



Strong Preference

Experience with China-based suppliers and international ocean freight

Familiarity with FBA reimbursement processes

Experience scaling logistics for 100+ variations (color/size matrix)

Understanding of how PPC and promotional activity affect inventory planning



You’ll thrive here if you:

Treat the department’s financial exposure like it’s your own money—you don’t wait to be told something is a problem

Instinctively create repeatable processes rather than solving the same problem manually each time

Can look at a list of urgent items and pick the ones that actually matter this week, then communicate clearly about what’s being deferred and why

This Isn’t For You If

Your strength is strategy and frameworks but you’re not excited about personally pulling data and building spreadsheets—at least until the team grows

You need heavy top-down direction to know what to work on, or you need perfect information before you’re willing to make a call

Your experience is entirely in enterprise or Fortune 500 logistics without e-commerce or Amazon exposure

You see a small, early-stage team and think “that’s beneath me” rather than “that’s a chance to build something”



WHAT YOU'LL GET

Competitive salary based on experience

Fully remote position. Work from anywhere

Direct access to the CEO. Your ideas and analysis reach the decision-maker, not a middle manager

Advanced tooling and infrastructure, including AI-powered analytics, custom dashboards, and automated reporting that let you focus on strategy instead of data entry

A team that values data-driven decision-making and continuous improvement

Opportunities for growth as the company expands into new marketplaces and channels

Employee discounts on Inspiratek products



HOW TO APPLY

Send your cover letter and CV to 
 using this subject line format:

Full Name - Country - Logistics and Supply Chain Manager - Source

(e.g., Jane Doe - United States - Logistics and Supply Chain Manager - LinkedIn)



Also submit along with your cover letter a brief response (3–5 sentences) to the following question:

Describe a time you inherited a logistics or supply chain function with no documented processes and built the operating system from scratch. What did you prioritize first, and why?



Applications that do not follow this subject line format will not be processed.

title: Web & Graphic Designer
description:
Location: Remote 

Company: Diversified Group with operations in Australia, China, and Hong Kong  

Industries: Professional Services, Agriculture

Job Type: Full-time, Permanent  

Salary: Competitive package commensurate with experience 



About Us  



We are a dynamic and diversified group running two distinct businesses across the Asia-Pacific region: 



- A Professional Accountancy & Advisory Firm with offices in Sydney, Shenzhen, and Hong Kong. 



- Strawberry Farms in Victoria and Queensland, Australia. 



 

We are looking for a Web & Graphic Designer to join our marketing team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for creating visually compelling designs and supporting the development and maintenance of websites for our brands and clients. 



This role focuses primarily on graphic design and web design/development, while also supporting marketing initiatives through creative assets, landing pages, and digital content. 



If you are interested in working with international brands, developing practical design and web skills, and contributing to high-impact marketing campaigns, we’d love to hear from you. 



Role & Responsibilities 



The responsibilities of this role are varied and involve both creative and technical tasks. While experience in some areas is preferred, we are happy to provide training for the right candidate. 



Key responsibilities include: 



- Design high-quality visual assets for digital use (social media, ads, websites, presentations, and marketing materials) 



- Create and maintain website pages and landing pages (UI/UX design and basic front-end implementation) 



- Ensure all designs are aligned with brand guidelines and visual identity 



- Collaborate with the marketing team to produce creatives for campaigns and promotions 



- Design and optimize landing pages to improve user experience and conversion rates 



- Assist in updating and maintaining website content (text, images, layout) 



- Develop wireframes, mockups, and prototypes for web pages and campaigns 



- Optimize designs for responsiveness across desktop, tablet, and mobile devices 



- Work with tools such as Adobe Creative Suite, Canva, or similar platforms 



- Support basic front-end development tasks (e.g., HTML/CSS edits, CMS updates like WordPress or Webflow) 



- Ensure fast-loading, visually appealing, and user-friendly web experiences 



- Stay up to date with the latest design trends, tools, and web technologies 



- Collaborate with internal teams to ensure designs meet marketing and business objectives 



Preferred Skills:



- Strong graphic design skills with a solid portfolio (digital and web projects) 



- Proficiency in design tools such as Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator, Canva or similar 



- Basic knowledge of web design principles (UI/UX, responsive design) 



- Familiarity with CMS platforms (e.g., WordPress, Webflow, Shopify) is a plus 



- Basic understanding of HTML/CSS is an advantage 



- Strong attention to detail and a good eye for aesthetics and layout 



- Good communication skills and ability to take feedback constructively 



- Organized and able to manage multiple design projects simultaneously 



- A proactive attitude and willingness to learn 



Requirements:



- 2–4+ years of experience in graphic design, web design, or a related role 



- Strong portfolio showcasing both graphic and web design work 



- Experience designing for digital platforms (websites, social media, ads, etc.) 



- Experience with website builders or CMS platforms 



- Understanding of design fundamentals (typography, color, layout, hierarchy) 



- Ability to translate marketing requirements into effective visual designs 



- Excellent English communication skills (written and verbal) 



- Experience working in a remote or collaborative team environment is an advantage 



- Bachelor’s degree in Design, Multimedia Arts, Computer Science, or a related field is preferred but not required 



 



What We Offer 



Diverse Challenge: Escape the monotony of a single industry. Apply your skills across three fascinating and different businesses. 



Professional Growth: This role reports directly to the Marketing Manager and offers significant scope for career advancement as the companies grow.  



Remote Flexibility: Work from anywhere in the Philippines with a stable internet connection.  



Competitive Compensation: A salary package that rewards expertise and results, plus opportunities for performance-based bonuses.  



Supportive Environment: You will have the full backing of the ownership team and access to the resources needed to succeed. 



 



How to Apply 



Ready to build something exceptional? Please send your application with the subject line: "Application: Web & Graphic Designer - [Your Name]". Your application must include: 



Your updated CV/Resume. 



A cover letter that addresses: 1. Why you are interested in creating graphics for two different businesses. 2. A brief example of a successful creative campaign you have led (mention the channels and key results). 3. Your salary expectation. 



(Optional but highly valued) Links to your portfolio, LinkedIn profile, or any relevant case studies. Applications without a cover letter will not be considered. 



We are an equal-opportunity employer and value diversity in our team.

title: Senior Amazon PPC Manager
description:
ABOUT US

Inspiratek LLC is a US-based e-commerce company generating $18M+ annually on Amazon, with expanding presence on TikTok Shop and DTC Shopify. We operate 5 private label brands with 20+ active products across the portfolio.

What makes us different: we're building one of the most data-driven operations in the private label space. Our team uses advanced analytics, AI-powered operational agents, custom data pipelines, and structured decision frameworks to manage every aspect of the business , from keyword-level PPC decisions to inventory forecasting to competitive intelligence. This isn't a company where you'll be making gut calls. Every decision is backed by data, and every action plan has measurable outcomes.

We're a 27-person distributed team across the world. We operate in a fast-paced, high-autonomy environment where ownership and accountability matter more than hours logged.



THE ROLE

We're hiring a Senior PPC Manager (Lead) to own the entire Amazon advertising function across our portfolio — 5 brands, 27+ products, and $180K+/month in ad spend. This is a leadership role: you will architect campaign strategy, lead the Amazon PPC team (currently 2 specialists), and drive measurable profitability improvements.

You will report directly to the CEO and work closely with Brand Managers and Launch Managers. The right candidate brings deep Amazon PPC expertise, financial discipline, and the ability to translate brand-level performance goals into campaign-level execution.

This is not a button-pushing role. We expect you to think in terms of profitability — not just ACOS — and to own the relationship between advertising spend, organic growth, and overall product economics.



WHAT YOU'LL OWN

Campaign Architecture & Execution

Design and manage campaign structures across Sponsored Products, Sponsored Brands (including Video), and Sponsored Display for the full portfolio

Structure campaigns by clear objectives — ranking, market share, defensive, conquesting, conversions, discovery, and branded

Maintain logical, consistent naming conventions across all campaigns

Optimize bids and placements using data — understand when and why to use Top-of-Search modifiers, Rest-of-Search adjustments, and Product Page placements

Manage campaign automation tools (we use Scale Insights) with the judgment to know when manual intervention is needed



Performance & Profitability Management

Own portfolio-level TACoS and per-product ACOS targets, always relative to break-even ACOS (not arbitrary fixed percentages)

Run regular PPC performance reviews — identify waste, find scaling opportunities, and optimize toward profitability

Understand how PPC performance connects to overall product economics — ad spend should drive profitable growth, not just revenue

Produce clear, concise performance reports for leadership



Brand Management ? PPC Coordination

Work closely with Brand Managers to align advertising tactics with brand performance goals

When brand-level analysis identifies specific performance issues (e.g., strong conversion but low visibility on key search terms), translate those findings into concrete PPC actions

Participate in regular cross-functional syncs to ensure PPC strategy supports — not conflicts with — broader brand strategy



Team Leadership

Lead, develop, and hold accountable 2 PPC specialists

Set weekly priorities, review work quality, and provide coaching

Own the PPC department's quarterly goals and bi-weekly sprint commitments

Build processes and guardrails that prevent costly mistakes (bid errors, budget overruns, campaign misconfigurations)



New Product Launch Support

Build and manage campaign structures for new product launches — understand that launch-phase PPC requires different targets, budgets, and patience than maintenance-phase products

Coordinate with Launch Managers on performance monitoring and budget phasing as products progress through early traction into sustainable organic growth

Know when a launch campaign is working, when it needs adjustment, and when to escalate



QUALIFICATIONS & REQUIREMENTS

Must Have

5+ years managing Amazon PPC campaigns (Sponsored Products, Sponsored Brands, Sponsored Display)

Proven experience managing $50K+/month in ad spend across multiple brands or products simultaneously

Deep understanding of ACOS, TACoS, break-even ACOS, and how advertising connects to product-level profitability

Experience structuring campaigns by objective — not just match type

Track record of improving advertising efficiency while maintaining or growing revenue

Team leadership experience — managing at least 1-2 PPC specialists or analysts

Advanced Excel/Google Sheets skills for data analysis and reporting

Fluent English communication (written and verbal) — daily async coordination with US-based CEO



Strong Preference

Experience with Scale Insights, Pacvue, or equivalent PPC automation tools

Experience in the bedding, home textiles, or home & kitchen category on Amazon

Familiarity with search term analysis, keyword grouping, and search volume-weighted performance aggregation

Experience with Helium 10, Data Dive, or similar Amazon research tools

Understanding of placement modifiers (Top-of-Search, Rest-of-Search, Product Pages) and when to use them strategically

Experience working with Amazon Brand Analytics or Search Query Performance data



Nice to Have

Experience with Amazon DSP or Amazon Marketing Cloud (AMC)

Experience with Walmart Connect or TikTok Shop advertising

Background in private label (different strategic approach than reselling or wholesale)

Multi-marketplace experience (US, CA, UK, EU)



WORKING CONDITIONS

100% remote — work from anywhere

Full-time (40 hours/week) with flexibility on daily schedule

Must overlap with US Eastern Time business hours for at least 4 hours daily (core hours: 10 AM – 2 PM EST recommended)

Company operates on a synchronized 2-week sprint cadence across all departments

Tools provided: Seller Central access, Scale Insights, Helium 10, Data Dive, SellerBoard



WHAT YOU'LL GET

Competitive salary based on experience

Fully remote position. Work from anywhere

Direct access to the CEO. Your ideas and analysis reach the decision-maker, not a middle manager

Advanced tooling and infrastructure, including AI-powered analytics, custom dashboards, and automated reporting that let you focus on strategy instead of data entry

A team that values data-driven decision-making and continuous improvement

Opportunities for growth as the company expands into new marketplaces and channels

Employee discounts on Inspiratek products



HOW TO APPLY

Send your cover letter and CV to 
 using this subject line format:

Full Name - Country - Senior PPC Manager - Source

(e.g., Jane Doe - United States - Senior PPC Manager - LinkedIn)



Applications that do not follow this subject line format will not be processed.

Thanks,? Sem Delver, Stimulicat

We support fast-paced, client-driven businesses by building efficient workflows and providing reliable marketing and operational support. As our partner brands continue to grow, we are expanding our marketing team to help scale paid acquisition and strengthen organic presence across social platforms.

The ideal candidate is data-minded, creative, and proactive, with a strong understanding of the Meta ads ecosystem and social media best practices. You will play a key role in helping our partner brands grow their audience, generate leads, and increase revenue through both paid and organic channels.

Paid Media Management 
 & Instagram Ads)

 and Instagram ad campaigns from start to finish

, Instagram, and other relevant platforms

Analytics & Reporting

, Instagram, and ideally TikTok)

Availability Requirements (Primary Hiring Focus):

eting (mandatory, 3pm CET)

title: Bookkeeper (Australia-Based Expertise)

title: Senior Amazon PPC Manager – Remote (Full-Time)
description:
Senior Amazon PPC Manager – Remote (Full-Time)



You live and breathe Amazon Ads. You've scaled accounts from 6 to 7 and 8 figures. You don't just "manage PPC" – you build strategies that crush the competition.



If that sounds like you, keep reading. If not, this role is not for you.



About Us

We are Fox Performance, an Amazon PPC agency founded in 2018. We work mainly with German clients, but also serve international brands across Europe and the USA. Our clients come from every industry you can imagine – supplements, beauty, home & living, pet, electronics, fashion, baby products, and more.



We are obsessed with Amazon. We test everything, read every update, break down every new beta feature, and push our clients to results they didn't think were possible. No autopilot. No "set and forget". No agency BS.

Now we are looking for a Senior Amazon PPC Manager – someone who can take full ownership of a portfolio of accounts, think strategically, and mentor junior tea
mbers along the way.



Who We Are NOT Looking For

Let's be direct. Do not apply if:



You are looking for a short-term gig or just a "bridge job" until something else comes up.

You are working multiple jobs in parallel and trying to squeeze us in on the side. We need your focus. Period.

You don't want to grow, learn, and push yourself every single week.

You want to clock in, clock out and never go beyond the bare minimum.

You see Amazon PPC as "just a job" instead of a craft you want to master.

You can't take direct, honest feedback without taking it personally.



We're not for everyone – and that's okay.

Who We ARE Looking For

We are looking for a true A-Player. Someone who:



Wants to go full throttle and be one of the best in the game.

Treats client accounts like their own business.

Is hungry, curious, and obsessed with getting better.

Wants to grow with us long-term and build a real career.

Brings energy, ideas, and ownership to the table every single day.



If that's you – we'd love to meet you.



What You'll Own



Full Account Ownership: manage a portfolio of premium client accounts end-to-end – from strategy to execution to client communication.

Advertising Strategy: build PPC strategies across Sponsored Products, Sponsored Brands, Sponsored Display – and where relevant, Amazon DSP and AMC.

Launch & Scale: from new product launches to aggressive scaling of existing winners – you know when to push and when to defend.

Advanced Optimization: bid strategies, placement optimization, SKU-level profitability, TACoS management, dayparting, portfolio structuring – you go way beyond dashboard-level.

Account Audits & Strategic Planning: diagnose weaknesses, identify opportunities, build 30/60/90 day roadmaps for every account.

Client Communication: lead client calls, present results, push back when needed, be the trusted expert clients rely on.

Mentorship: help our Junior PPC Managers and Assistants grow – share your playbook, review their work, develop the next generation.

Process Development: help us build better SOPs, templates and internal tools – your experience shapes how we operate.

Stay Ahead: test new Amazon features, evaluate new tools, and bring fresh ideas to the team.





What You Must Know (Non-Negotiables)

These are the things you absolutely need to have under your belt as a Senior:



Deep mastery of all Sponsored Ads formats: Sponsored Products, Sponsored Brands (incl. Video & Brand Store), Sponsored Display (incl. Audiences & Custom Creatives), and how they interact.

Campaign architecture: SKAG, single-keyword, theme-based, performance-based – you know which structure works when and why.

Advanced bidding strategies: dynamic bids, placement modifiers, top-of-search optimization, bid by placement, bid hijacking defense.

Keyword research & targeting: Helium 10 (Cerebro, Magnet, Frankenstein), Brand Analytics, Search Query Performance Report (SQP) – you can pull insights most managers miss.

TACoS vs ACoS: you understand the difference, when to optimize for which, and how to communicate it to clients.

Search Term Reports & Negative Mining: you build clean, performance-focused accounts without keyword cannibalization.

Brand Defense & Conquesting: you protect your client's branded search and attack competitors strategically.

Organic & Paid Synergy: you understand how PPC influences organic ranking (and vice versa) – BSR, indexation, halo effect.

Listing Fundamentals: you know that even the best PPC can't fix a bad listing – and you can identify what needs to change.

Reporting & Storytelling: you don't just deliver numbers – you tell the story behind them.

Bulk File Operations: you can edit, optimize and upload bulk files like a surgeon.

Tool Stack: Helium 10, Brand Analytics, Pacvue / Perpetua / Adtomic / Sellerboard or similar – at least one professional ad management tool you've worked with hands-on.

Bonus (high value): Amazon DSP, AMC (Amazon Marketing Cloud), Vendor Central, international marketplaces (US, UK, DE, FR, IT, ES).



If you read this list and thought "yes, that's me" – continue. If half of these are unfamiliar, this role is not the right fit yet.



What You Bring



4+ years of hands-on Amazon Advertising experience (agency or in-house), ideally managing multiple 6- and 7-figure accounts.

Proven track record of scaling Amazon accounts profitably – with real case studies you can walk us through.

Expert-level data analysis skills – Excel / Google Sheets is your playground; pivot tables, XLOOKUP, conditional logic are second nature.

Strategic mindset – you think in frameworks, not just dashboards.

Excellent English (spoken and written). German is a big plus, but not required.

Strong communication – confident in client calls, clear in writing, direct but professional.

Ownership mentality – fully accountable, no excuses.

Stable internet and a professional remote work setup.





What We Offer

You already know the foundation we offer (100% remote, MacBook, full-time, long-term, competitive USD salary, after one year: 13th month pay, 20 days PTO, paid sick leave, Philippine holidays...).





Ownership & Growth



Clear path to Team Lead / Head of PPC – we are growing, and senior hires are who we promote from.

Voice in strategy – you help shape how the agency operates.

Direct line to leadership – no middle management, no politics.

Conference & event budget – Amazon Unboxed, PPC events, industry conferences – we send you.

Annual learning budget – courses, certifications, books, coaches – you choose.



Time & Space to Do Great Work



Flexible hours – core overlap with German business hours (2–3 hours/day), the rest is yours.

Deep Work culture – no unnecessary meetings, no micromanagement.

Real team culture – weekly team calls, virtual coffee, and annual team events.



If you read the full text start your message with "HI WOLF"

mbers and help shape best practices for the

 programs and automation workflows.

 platforms (e.g., Klaviyo, Attentive).

 is a plus.

title: Digital Marketing and Tech Savvy VA
description:
We run a finance education and strategic finance business with a large LinkedIn audience. We're hiring a Digital Marketing and Tech Savvy VA who can operate the full marketing engine day-to-day: newsletters,
 sequences, campaigns, social publishing, community engagement, lead magnets, landing pages, automations, and reporting.



We need an operator with a solid track record, strong judgment, tight execution, and high standards.



What you’ll own:



1.
 + beehiiv



Draft and edit newsletters (tight, precise, finance-literate writing).



Build and manage
 sequences, automations, segmentation, tagging, and campaign sends in beehiiv.

Maintain list hygiene and deliverability basics (engagement-based segmentation, clean sending practices).

Track performance (open rate, CTR, conversions) and recommend improvements.



2. Socials + repurposing



Repurpose long-form content into platform-native posts (LinkedIn pages + other platforms).

Schedule, publish, and QA posts (formatting, links, tracking, assets).

Manage community posts and engagement cadence.

Use platform tools to drive engagement (ManyChat and other platform-specific tools where appropriate).



3. Lead magnets + assets + design coordination



Propose new lead magnet ideas aligned to the audience and products.

Coordinate creation of lead magnets and post visuals with the designer (briefs, iteration loops, final QA).

Maintain an organized asset library and content calendar.



4. Web + landing pages + SEO



Create and manage landing pages for lead magnets and offers (copy updates, embeds, forms, HTML edits if needed).

Basic SEO implementation (titles, meta descriptions, on-page updates).

Track performance in Google Analytics and deliver a simple weekly report with insights.



5. Automations



Build and maintain 
 automations (and Zapier when needed).

Connect forms, tagging, sequences, and reporting with reliable logic and error handling.

Document what you build so it’s maintainable.



6. Non-negotiables



Excellent editor. You can tighten copy without changing meaning, introducing errors, or adding fluff.

Finance-literate. You understand basic finance/accounting language and can keep it accurate.

Strong beehiiv skills. You have actually built sequences, segmentation, automations, and campaigns.

Digital Margeting expertise. You understand funnel building, conversion tracking, segmentation, engagement, etc

Reliable execution. You hit deadlines, QA your work, and follow systems.

High integrity: confidentiality, discretion, loyalty. 

Responsible AI use. You can use AI to accelerate drafts, but you do not invent facts, stats, or claims. Anything factual must be verified and sourced properly. No confidential uploads into unapproved tools.



Tools you should be comfortable with



beehiiv 

Canva 


 and Zapier

HTML 

Gamma app 

Google Analytics and basic SEO

ManyChat 

Notion

ClickUp 



How we work



You’ll receive priorities and voice guidelines, then you run execution with minimal hand-holding.

Weekly live (PST availability) + async work

Clear file naming, version control, and organized folders are mandatory.

You flag issues early and propose solutions.



Trial assignment - Live on camera



Final candidates will complete a paid test that includes:



Editing a newsletter draft 

Building a simple beehiiv flow from a short spec (tagging + segmentation + sequence)

Repurposing one LinkedIn post into 2–3 platform-native variants



To apply share your linkedin, work samples as appicable and availability for a live test

title: Wholesale Assistant- Fashion/ Clothing Brand
description:

This role is ideal for someone with strong admin skills, excellent communication, and the ability to keep orders, product information, and customer correspondence accurate and on track.



This is a remote role requiring someone who can work confidently, communicate professionally, and provide reliable day-to-day support across wholesale operations.

- Assist with wholesale order processing, updates, and maintenance

- Manage customer communications in a professional and timely manner

- Support account coordination and respond to client enquiries

title: ML Data Labeler (Aviation / Flight Logbooks)
description:
## Overview




 is hiring a **detail-oriented ML Data Labeler** to help us improve AI systems that process aviation data.



You’ll review **flight data logbooks**, label and organize information, and verify that our AI-generated **JSON output** matches the source data correctly. You’ll also help build simple **evals/testing workflows** in tools like **LangSmith** and **Braintrust**.



This is a great fit for someone who is careful, organized, and comfortable working with structured data. You do **not** need to be a full engineer, but you should be somewhat technical and comfortable learning new tools.



## What You’ll Do



- Review and label aviation-related data, scanned documents, and especially **flight logbooks**

- Compare source inputs against AI-generated **JSON output**

- Check for accuracy, missing fields, formatting issues, and inconsistencies

- Flag bad outputs and provide clear notes on what went wrong

- Help create and maintain **evals** and testing datasets in platforms like **LangSmith** and **Braintrust**

- Work closely with our team to improve data quality and model performance

- Follow labeling guidelines and help refine them over time



## Requirements



- Strong attention to detail

- Good written English

- Comfortable working with spreadsheets, structured data, and repetitive review tasks

- Able to compare source documents against JSON or other structured outputs

- Basic technical comfort level: you don’t need to code heavily, but you should not be intimidated by JSON, QA workflows, or AI tools

- Reliable internet and ability to work independently



## Nice to Have



- Familiarity with **aviation**, flight operations, or pilot logbooks

- Experience with **data labeling**, QA, or annotation

- Experience using tools like **LangSmith**, **Braintrust**, or similar AI evaluation platforms

- Basic understanding of **JSON**

- Experience reviewing model outputs for consistency and correctness



## Ideal Candidate



- Very detail-oriented and consistent

- Comfortable spotting small errors

- Interested in AI / machine learning workflows

- Can follow instructions carefully and ask good questions when something is unclear

- Bonus if you have aviation exposure, but it is not required



## Compensation



- Hourly or part-time to start

- Potential for ongoing work if it’s a good fit



## To Apply



Please send:



1. A short introduction about yourself

2. Any experience with data labeling, QA, aviation, or JSON

3. Examples of tools you’ve used (spreadsheets, annotation tools, LangSmith, Braintrust, etc.)

4. Your hourly rate

5. Your availability



Also include the phrase **“Radar Ready”** in your application so we know you read this fully.

title: Performance Media Buyer — Meta Ads & Google Ads
description:
ABOUT KINETIC MARKETING CO

Kinetic Marketing Co is an Australian digital marketing agency specialising in paid advertising, SEO, and marketing automation for trade and home improvement businesses. We work with clients across Melbourne and the wider Australian market, running Meta Ads and Google Ads campaigns that generate real leads — for electricians, builders, outdoor living companies, and more.



We are a small, fast-moving team that values ownership, transparency, and results. We use ClickUp for task management, GoHighLevel for client CRM and funnels, and Slack for communication. We are growing and we want you to grow with us.



THE ROLE

We are looking for a full-time, experienced performance media buyer to own and optimise paid advertising across our entire client portfolio. This is not a junior role. You will be inside ad accounts every day, making independent decisions, testing creative, diagnosing performance issues, and improving results without waiting to be told what to do.



You will work directly with the agency founder and report to him weekly. The founder will maintain visibility into all accounts — you are here to drive performance, not just execute instructions.



WHAT YOU WILL BE DOING

-Managing Meta Ads and Google Ads campaigns across multiple client accounts in the trade and home service niche

-Monitoring daily performance — spend, CPL, CTR, ROAS — and making proactive optimisations without being asked

-Setting up new campaigns from scratch including audience research, campaign structure, ad copy direction, and conversion tracking

-Running A/B tests on creative, copy, and audiences — documenting results and iterating based on data

-Sending a clear weekly performance report to the founder covering every active client account — results, observations, and what you are doing about it

-Flagging performance issues immediately with a plan — not after the fact, not at the end of the week

-Keeping all tasks updated in ClickUp and submitting a brief end-of-day summary each working day

-Staying across platform updates, algorithm changes, and best practices — and applying them proactively



WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

-Minimum 3 years of hands-on experience running Meta Ads and Google Ads — agency experience strongly preferred

-Proven experience managing lead generation campaigns for trade, home service, or local service businesses — not just ecommerce

-Deep understanding of campaign structures — CBO vs ABO on Meta, Search vs PMAX on Google, bidding strategies, and audience segmentation

-Solid grasp of conversion tracking — GTM, Meta Pixel, Google Ads conversion actions. You know why it matters and how to verify it is working

-Excellent written English — your weekly reports must be clear, concise, and insight-driven, not just a data dump

-Proactive communicator — you flag problems early, ask when blocked, and never go quiet on an underperforming account

-Ability to manage multiple accounts simultaneously without needing daily micromanagement

-Availability to overlap with Australian Eastern Standard Time (AEST) for at least 2–3 hours per day



NICE TO HAVE

-Experience with GoHighLevel (GHL) or similar CRM and funnel platforms

-Familiarity with Google Tag Manager beyond basic installation

-Experience working with Australian clients or understanding of the Australian market

-Experience setting up and managing Google Ads call extensions and call tracking



WHAT WE OFFER

Full-time role — upto 40 hours per week, fully remote

Direct working relationship with the agency founder — no layers of management, no bureaucracy

Performance visibility — you will see the direct impact of your work on client results

A growing agency with real ambition — room to take on more responsibility as the business scales



HOW TO APPLY — READ THIS CAREFULLY

We receive a high volume of applications. To be considered, you must answer all five screening questions below in full. Applications that skip questions or do not follow the instructions will not be reviewed — regardless of experience.



1.  Describe a Meta Ads or Google Ads campaign you have managed for a trade or home service business. What was the objective, what was the CPL when you started, and what did you bring it to?

2.  A client's Meta campaign has been running for 3 weeks. Spend is on track but leads have dropped significantly in the last 5 days. Walk me through exactly how you would diagnose and respond to this.

3.  What does your weekly reporting process look like? What metrics do you include and how do you present findings to a founder or client?

4.  How many ad accounts are you currently managing and across what platforms? What is the approximate total monthly ad spend you are responsible for?

5.  What is your availability overlap with Australian Eastern Standard Time (AEST) and how many hours per week can you commit to this role?

title: Livestream Operations Manager (onsite at Parañaque)
description:
Dear Applicants,



## Livestream Operations Manager – We're Hiring! ##



**Please note that this is an onsite job at Parañaque**



We run live commerce operations across multiple countries and platforms, and we need an experienced Livestream Operations Manager to keep everything running smoothly. You'll manage session flow, lead and support the hosting team, onboard new livestreamers, and step on camera yourself when needed.



IMPORTANT: This role requires prior experience in both livestreaming and operations management. You must be confident on camera and equally confident running the show behind it.



Job Scope:

- Coordinate and manage livestream schedules across platforms and markets

- Ensure smooth execution of sessions — briefing hosts, monitoring quality, and troubleshooting in real time

- Sub in as a livestream host when needed

- Onboard and guide new livestreamers on workflows, platform tools, brand standards, and live selling techniques

- Monitor concurrent live streams and provide real-time support to hosts

- Track performance metrics and compile regular reports with insights

- Maintain and improve SOPs for all livestream operations

- Coordinate with marketing, content, and supply chain teams to align promotions and logistics



Requirements:

- Prior experience as a livestream host or live commerce presenter — confident and comfortable on camera

- Prior experience in livestream operations or e-commerce operations management

- Able to onboard and coach new livestreamers effectively

- Familiar with TikTok Shop, Shopee Live, or similar live selling platforms

- Strong organisational and multi-tasking skills

- Good written and spoken English

- Proactive and solutions-focused with a high sense of ownership



Note:

- Full-time basis – 6 hrs/day (with 1 hr break), 6 working days within a Monday–Sunday window

- Shifts are between 8am and 10pm (Philippine Standard Time) — specific schedule to be discussed

- 14 days of Paid Annual Leave and Paid Public Holidays (all major holidays in the Philippines) included



IMPORTANT: When applying, please provide:

- Your CV/Resume

- A short video introduction of yourself and why you think you're a good fit

- Why you are leaving your last job or what you were doing just before this application

- A portfolio or examples of previous livestream hosting and/or operations management work — this can include links to live sessions, screenshots of dashboards, SOPs, or team coordination work you've handled. Applications without demonstrated livestream experience will not be considered.



Applications without the above points will be less considered.



P.S. Please format your replies in point form according to the items asked above, e.g.:

• My resume is here:

• Video introduction here:

• I am currently doing... / I am leaving because...

• Here are examples of my work...



The more information you provide in your initial application, the better.



Best regards,

Loo Yi

THE ROLE

You’ll have access to a well-built LinkedIn profile and your job is simple to define and hard to fake: get quality meetings on the calendar. No cold calling. Human, relationship-first outreach — the kind that actually works.

title: Video Editor — Escape Agency Burnout • One Brand, World-Class Standards
description:
You’ve done the agency thing. Fast turnarounds, demanding clients, and a culture that rewards hours logged over quality produced. You’re tired.

You want a role where quality actually matters. Where “good enough” isn’t good enough. Where you can slow down enough to do genuinely excellent work — and be part of a team that appreciates it.

This is that role.

You’ll produce 4–6 long-form videos per month (10–60 minutes each) — YouTube, course content, webinars — with clear briefs, direct creative collaboration, and enough time to do it properly. You’re our kind of person if you deliver excellent work without creating friction, take ownership without needing applause, and genuinely celebrate when a teammate wins.

High Performance. Zero Drama. Peaceful & Profitable — we mean all three.



High standards, sustainable pace. This is your sanctuary from agency burnout.

?

title: Web / Landing Page Designer- DTC & Conversion-Focused
description:
Please complete this form: 



About Us



We're  a fast-growing DTC e-commerce brand selling pheromone fragrances and skincare products across the US and Southeast Asian markets. We run paid social at scale, and our entire growth engine runs through our funnels. We're not a typical brand team. We're operators who move fast, test constantly, and care deeply about what works.

We're building out our in-house creative performance and web team, and we're looking for a Web Designer who can hold their own in a high-performance environment where your designs are measured by conversion rates, not just how good they look.

 

The Role



This is a full-time contractor position where you'll own the design of our prelanders and product pages across multiple DTC brands. You'll move from freelance support to a core tea
mber - someone who's in the feedback loop, understands what we're trying to achieve on every page, and ships fast without sacrificing quality.

You'll work closely with the CEO, Head of Retention,  to the Head of Marketing.

 

What You'll Be Doing

•    Designing high-converting prelanders, landing pages, and product pages for Goda and some of our internal brand portfolio as needed

•    Iterating on designs based on A/B test results and performance data

•    Ensure the page designs cohesively match the ad creative driving traffic

•    Collaborating in a real feedback loop with the team ( sharing WIPs, incorporating notes quickly, and flagging blockers early)

•    Operating within our design system and brand guidelines while pushing quality forward

•    Coordinating with our developer to get pages built and live efficiently

•    Maintaining consistent output volume while hitting quality benchmarks

 

What We're Looking For



Must-Haves

•    Solid years of design experience with a strong, verifiable portfolio

•    Deep understanding of conversion-focused design - you think about the buyer journey, not just aesthetics

•    Proficiency in Figma (primary tool), Photoshop, and AI design tools

•    Genuine understanding of how DTC marketing works & you know what a funnel is and why it matters

•    Strong grasp of copywriting principles, you can spot when copy and design aren't working together

•    Willingness to give and receive direct feedback as part of your regular workflow



Instant No's

•    No understanding of conversion or what drives people to buy

•    Not a team player- if you prefer to work in isolation and skip the feedback loop, this isn't the right fit

•    No DTC or performance marketing experience

 

What the First 90 Days Look Like



First 30 Days

•    Complete onboarding and brand immersion

•    Produce your first batch of designs and begin understanding our brand voices and customer avatars

•    Deliver consistently and show up reliably in the feedback cycle



60 Days

•    Working with minimal revision rounds

•    Supporting the Marketing Department with cohesive landing page designs that match ad creative

•    Starting to influence conversion rate through design decisions



90 Days

•    Independently designing and delivering winning prelanders

•    A reliable, trusted contributor in our marketing cycles

•    High-volume output with strong quality-to-speed ratio

 

How We Measure Success

•    Conversion rate improvement on the pages you design

•    A/B test contribution and learnings

•    Responsiveness, adherence to SOPs, and turnaround time

 

Compensation & Benefits

•    Competitive renumeration

•    Fully remote, no office, no commute, no fixed timezone

•    Learning & development budget

•    Access to premium design and marketing tools

•    Clear growth path: Web Designer ? Lead Web Designer

•    Stable, multi-brand portfolio with real creative ambition

•    Coaching from a high-performing senior team

•    Weekly performance reviews and access to winning ad references

 

???? This role is ideal for a designer who is done doing pretty work that doesn't perform. If you've always wanted to be part of a DTC team where your output is directly tied to revenue, this is it.



Please complete this form:

title: Level 1 MSP Support Technician (Behind the Scenes) - Australian MSP
description:
Hi, I'm Brett, Director of 4iT Support, a managed IT services company based in Sydney, Australia. We look after IT support, Microsoft 365, networking, and cybersecurity for small and medium businesses across Sydney. I'm looking for a full time Level 1 Support Technician to work behind the scenes with our local team.



This is NOT a phone role. You will NOT be on calls with clients. Your work is entirely
, ticket, and Microsoft Teams based. We handle the phone side; you handle the written side.



What you will be doing

- Triaging new tickets in our PSA - reading them, asking the right clarifying questions, and either resolving or escalating

- First response and resolution on common Microsoft 365 issues: user setup, licence assignment, password resets, MFA, shared mailboxes, distribution lists, Teams and SharePoint basics

- Writing up clear, well documented ticket notes so the rest of the team can pick up where you left off

- Helping keep our client documentation tidy and up to date

- Escalating anything you can't solve to our Level 2 team with enough context that they can run with it



Over time, as you grow into the role, we will train you on our full behind the scenes toolkit including vendor portals (Sophos, Pax8, Cloudflare, and others), licence management, and the admin work that keeps our clients secure. We want someone eager to keep learning, not someone who only wants to do the same task every day.



What we are looking for

- Clear, professional written English - this is the single most important skill

- At least 2 years experience in an MSP, internal IT helpdesk, or similar technical support role

- Solid working knowledge of Microsoft 365 administration (Entra ID / Azure AD, Exchange Online, Teams, SharePoint)

- Comfortable troubleshooting Windows desktop issues

- Basic networking understanding (DNS, DHCP, IP addressing, VPN basics)

- Experience with any PSA / RMM platform - we use Syncro, but if you know ConnectWise, Atera, NinjaOne, Kaseya, Halo, or similar, you will pick up Syncro quickly

- Comfortable documenting your work properly

- A working computer running Windows or macOS

- A fast, stable, wired internet connection - minimum 25 Mbps down / 10 Mbps up on fibre or cable. No satellite, no mobile/pocket WiFi as your primary connection. Low latency matters more than raw speed for this role

- A backup plan for when your internet or power goes down (mobile hotspot, nearby cafe, etc.)

- A quiet place to work



How you will work

- We will provide you with a Windows 365 Cloud PC - this is your 4iT workstation, hosted in Microsoft's cloud, fully managed by us

- You will connect to your Cloud PC from your own device through the Windows App or a modern web browser. No VPN, no software installed on your personal machine

- All your work, files, logins, and tools live on the Cloud PC - not on your personal device. This keeps your personal data separate from work data

- This model means your internet connection quality directly affects your ability to do the job. If your connection drops or lags, you cannot work

- All software, licences, and access you need are provided by us on the Cloud PC



Working hours

- Monday to Friday, 9:30am to 6:30pm Sydney time, year round

In Philippine time that is 7:30am to 4:30pm PHT during Australian winter (April to October) and 6:30am to 3:30pm PHT during Australian summer (October to April)

- 1 hour lunch break, flexible between 12pm and 2pm Sydney time

- This is a 40 hour week of actual work



Compensation and benefits

- $800 USD per month to start

- Salary review at 90 days, moving to $900 USD per month on satisfactory performance

- Paid fortnightly

- 13th month bonus paid in December, pro rated from your start date in your first year

- Philippine public holidays observed

- No strict cap on time off - just give us reasonable notice

- All tools provided: Microsoft 365 account, Syncro, Claude AI, 1Password, training resources



What we offer beyond the pay

- Long term full time role - we want someone who is with us for years, not months

- Clear training and documentation, not sink or swim

- Genuine support when you get stuck

- Opportunity to grow into Level 2 work over time as you prove yourself



How to apply

- Please reply to this post with a short
 introducing yourself

- Tell me briefly about your current or most recent MSP or IT support role

- Tell me which PSA and RMM tools you have used, and for how long

- Tell me your internet speed (a speedtest.net screenshot is perfect) and whether your connection is fibre, cable, DSL, mobile, or something else

- Include the word "Parramatta" at the end of your subject line so I know you read the whole post

- Do NOT send me a long list of your skills or a generic CV paste - I would rather hear a few sentences from you in your own words



Looking forward to hearing from you.

Brett

Director, 4iT Support

Sydney, Australia

title: Senior Video Editor
description:
???? Senior Video Editor (Performance Creative)

This is a performance-driven role, not a time-based one.



We value results more than hours worked — that’s why we offer large performance bonuses. High-performing editors can earn merit-based bonuses up to $10,000.

3. Consistent first-edit approvals

4. Ownership, speed, and reliability



Bonuses are not tied to seniority or time spent online.



If you think like a growth partner, understand how ads convert, and take ownership of outcomes — this role offers real upside, not capped potential.



?? Read This First — Who This Role Is For



Apply only if you are:

1. Hungry to grow fast

2. Serious about long-term skill and income growth

3. Someone who values performance over hours worked

4. Comfortable in a high-expectation, fast-moving environment





We are a performance marketing agency managing multiple e-commerce brands and have generated over USD $10,000,000 in total revenue.



Our growth is rapid.

Our standards are high.

Our ability to adapt quickly is critical.



This role is not for beginners.



That said — you will not be left alone.



We have senior-level strategists and operators who actively review work, give feedback, and help you grow into a top-tier performance editor.



If you want:

Stability without growth ? this is not the role

Comfort without accountability ? this is not the role



If you want to compound skills, income, and career trajectory — keep reading.



???? Overview

Your job is to turn winning scripts and concepts into performance-ready ads that hit hard within the first few seconds and convert on the back end.



You’re not just editing videos — you’re building ads that are made to scale.



This means:

- Hitting the hook fast

- Keeping the viewer engaged throughout

- Bringing product and outcome to life visually

- Matching the format to the platform (Meta, TikTok, YouTube)





Experience & Fit (Read Carefully)

This role is not suitable for junior editors, social media editors, or branding-only creatives.



1. Deep Understanding of Human Psychology (Required)

? You understand how viewers emotionally process ads, not just how to edit clips.

? You know how to use visual pacing, sequencing, and framing to guide emotion

? You can translate fear, relief, curiosity, urgency, desire, and trust into visuals

? You actively think about what the viewer feels second-by-second



2. 3–5+ Years in the E-commerce Editing Industry (Non-Negotiable)

? You have at least 3–5 years of experience in editing e-commerce ads.

? You’ve edited ads for products that sell online, not just content for views

? You understand funnels, presell logic, and why certain creatives scale



3. E-commerce Performance Editors Only

? Paid social ads (Meta, TikTok, YouTube Shorts)

? UGC-style conversion creatives

? Performance-focused editing (hooks, retention, CTAs)



If your background is mainly:

? Music videos

? Corporate videos

? Brand films

? Long-form YouTube



???? This role is not a match.



4. English Proficiency (Required)

? Fluent or strong working proficiency in English

? Able to understand scripts without hand-holding

? Can match captions, overlays, and pacing to English VO naturally

? Sensitive to tone, urgency, and emotional nuance



???? What You Need to Be Good At

? Understanding what makes an ad convert — especially in the first 3–6 seconds

? Knowing how to pace, cut, and sequence to match the creative strategist’s script

? Being fast without sacrificing structure

? Turning raw content (UGC, testimonials, overlays) into clean ad flows

? Spotting when a visual slows the message and fixing it independently



???? KPIs

? Final ads delivered per week: 18–27 finished video ads (with variations)

? Hook variations delivered per week: Minimum 3 hook cuts from the same script

? Thumb-stop rate (0–3s retention): ? 30%

? Edit approval rate (1st version): ? 80?cepted without major changes

? Re-edit turnaround time: ? 24 hours

? Raw assets tagged and uploaded: 100% to Google Drive B-Roll folder



???? Daily Tasks

? Check for new scripts and concepts from Creative Strategist

? Review brief, script, and reference ads

? Request missing footage or raw files if needed

? Cut 2-3 CONCEPTs/day depending on complexity

? Use platform-first pacing (hook in first 0.5–1.5s)

? Add captions, overlays, and brand assets

? Save files in final output folder and update naming convention

?Upload all trimmed/raw B-roll used to shared Drive



???? Weekly Tasks

? Deliver 5–10 final edited concepts by Friday

? Build at least 5 hook variations per script

? Review ad performance with Creative Strategist

? Reverse-engineer top-performing videos

? Tag and organize raw content used in edits



???? Collaboration

? Align with Creative Strategist on angle and awareness level

? Stay updated on hook formats that are working

?Sync weekly with strategist and media buyer

? Propose improvements proactively



???? Non-Negotiables

? No unlabelled videos or dumped B-roll

? No edits launched without hook clarity

? You are not just editing — you are building a winning ad system

? Hit your weekly quota and own quality

? Stay curious and reverse-engineer winning Meta/TikTok ads

title: Shopify Store Builder
description:
Store Builder (E-commerce Landing Pages) — Barefoot Footwear Brand



Performance Bonus (Results-Based)

???? $100–$1,000 bonus per winning landing page built

This is a performance-driven role, not a time-based one.

We reward results, not hours worked.

High-performing store builders can earn merit-based bonuses up to $1,000 per winning landing page, depending on:

?Page complexity

?Quality of execution

?Conversion performance (“winning degree”)



If you think like a growth partner, understand how landing pages convert, and take ownership of outcomes — this role has real upside and uncapped potential.



Role Overview

We are actively expanding our product line in the barefoot footwear niche, and we need a store builder who can create high-converting product pages daily.

You will be provided with a product listing link. Your job is to:

?Pull the product images and information from the link

?Improve the presentation (branding + structure)

?Build the product page on our website using our drag-and-drop template

?Write persuasive copy and optimize the page for conversions



As you improve, we will also provide training to build more advanced funnels such as:

?Quizzes

?Listicles

?Advertorial-style pages

?Presell funnels



What You Must Be Good At

You should already be strong in:

1) Copywriting & Buyer Psychology

You understand how customers think, what they care about, and what drives purchase decisions.

You can write copy that:

?Makes benefits clear

?Builds trust

?Handles objections

?Justifies the price point

?Moves the customer toward action



2) Graphic Design & Image Editing

You can edit product images so they look premium and branded (not cheap or generic).

This includes:

?Improving layout and visual hierarchy

?Creating clean, conversion-friendly image sections

?Basic product image retouching and design polish

?Using AI tools to enhance assets (example: changing backgrounds, improving image quality, creating clean product visuals).



3) Strong Marketing Fundamentals

This is not “copy-paste product listing work.”

You must know how to take a basic product listing and turn it into a branded, conversion-optimized landing page that sells.



Experience & Fit (Read Carefully)

This role is not suitable for beginners or junior store builders who only upload products and paste descriptions.

We are looking for someone who can build pages like a marketer, not like a data entry worker.

Requirements

? 2–5+ years of e-commerce experience

? You’ve built product pages for other brands

? You understand funnels, presell logic, and conversion strategy

? Strong English proficiency (you can clearly tell good copy from bad copy)



Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)

???? 8–10 product pages delivered per week (depending on complexity)



Key Responsibilities

Daily:

?Review the product link provided

?Create a full product page on our website

?Write conversion-focused copy

?Edit images to look branded and premium

?Ensure the page structure is clean and optimized for sales





Weekly / Monthly

?Work directly with the Marketing Director to prioritize products and page builds

?Join weekly/monthly reviews to improve page quality and conversion rate

?Identify areas to improve (copy, layout, visuals, clarity, offer presentation)



Who This Role Is Perfect For

You’ll do well here if you:

?Take ownership and care about performance

?Understand how ads connect to landing page conversion

?Can build pages fast without sacrificing quality

?Want a role where results are rewarded with real bonuses
