title: Email Marketing Manager
description:
Reformly is a fast-growing home Pilates brand helping women aged 35–65 feel stronger, healthier, and more confident from home.



Our mission is simple: give women everything they need to feel strong, confident, and unstoppable.



We’re a tight-knit team of 12 full-time people who move fast, care deeply about quality, and genuinely love what we build. Every person who joins Reformly gets the opportunity to grow with us long-term. We’re not hiring for a quick project or an agency. We’re looking for someone who wants to be part of something bigger and help shape our culture as we scale.



The Role



We’re looking for a full-time
 Marketing Manager who will take ownership of our
 marketing channel from strategy to execution.



This is not a basic newsletter role. This is not a “send a few campaigns every week” role.



You’ll be the person responsible for building and optimizing high-performing flows, creating strong campaigns, segmenting our audience intelligently, and driving as much revenue as possible from
 without damaging list quality or brand experience.



You should already have strong hands-on experience with Klaviyo and know how to use
 as a serious growth channel. You understand how to balance performance with brand, how to keep a list healthy while still pushing revenue, and how to create campaigns that feel relevant, well-timed, and aligned with the customer journey.



You’ll also need to be highly skilled in Figma, with the ability to create polished, on-brand
 designs that match Reformly’s visual identity and speak directly to our target audience.



This role requires both strategic thinking and strong execution. You’ll work closely with the team to plan campaigns, improve flows, test ideas, refine segmentation, and make sure every
 sent serves a clear purpose.



What You’ll Own



•
 flows: building, optimizing, and maintaining key Klaviyo flows across the customer journey 

• Campaign strategy: planning and executing campaigns that drive revenue while staying aligned with the brand 

• Segmentation: creating smart audience segments based on behavior, engagement, lifecycle stage, and purchase intent 

• List health: managing deliverability, engagement, suppression, and overall list quality 

• Revenue growth: finding opportunities to increase
-attributed revenue without hurting customer experience 

•
 design: creating high-quality, on-brand
 creatives in Figma 

• Testing and optimization: improving subject lines, content, timing, offers, segmentation, and creative performance 

• Brand alignment: making sure all
 communication feels consistent with Reformly’s tone, identity, and positioning



What We’re Looking For



Must-have



• Proven experience in
 marketing, ideally for e-commerce or direct-to-consumer brands 

• Strong hands-on experience with Klaviyo 

• Deep understanding of
 flows, campaigns, segmentation, and list management 

• Strong knowledge of how to grow revenue through
 while protecting deliverability and list health 

• Expert-level Figma skills 

• Ability to create
s that are not only visually strong, but also strategically built to convert 

• Strong understanding of customer psychology, retention, and lifecycle marketing 

• Clear communication skills and ability to collaborate smoothly with the wider team 

• High attention to detail and strong execution skills



Nice to have



• Experience in health, wellness, fitness, or women-focused brands 

• Strong instinct for brand storytelling and campaign messaging 

• Experience with A/B testing and performance analysis 

• Ability to manage multiple campaigns and priorities in a fast-paced environment 

• A self-driven mindset with a strong focus on growth, quality, and continuous improvement



Why Reformly



• Full-time, long-term opportunity in a company that’s growing fast 

• Real ownership over one of the most important revenue channels in the business 

• Direct collaboration with a highly involved team that cares deeply about quality and execution 

• The chance to grow with the company and play a meaningful role in shaping the brand as we scale 

• Your voice matters here, and your growth matters just as much as the company’s



How to Apply



Video applications only.



Please send us:



• A short introduction video telling us about yourself



• Your experience with Klaviyo, flows, campaigns, and segmentation



• Examples of
 work or brands you’ve worked on that you’re most proud of



• A walkthrough of how you think about revenue, list health, and brand alignment in
 marketing



• Why you believe you’d be a strong fit for Reformly



This is not optional. Applications without a video will not be considered.

title: Motion.Page and Bricks Builder Specialist
description:
Position: Full-Time Website and Graphic Designer



Location: Remote/United Kingdom



About Growth Provision:



Growth Provision is a premier Marketing and Consulting agency based in the UK, specializing in serving clients within the financial services sector. We're seeking a talented motion.page and bricks builder specialist to help take our client's website to the next level. 



Role Overview:



We're on the hunt for a creative and tech-savvy Motion.page Specialist. In this role, you'll leverage Motion.page, the #1 WordPress Animation Plugin, to craft dynamic and interactive web experiences. From ScrollTrigger animations to engaging page transitions, your work will captivate our audience and set new standards for our digital content.



Key Responsibilities:



Design and implement animations using Motion.page to enhance user engagement across our WordPress sites.

Work closely with web designers and developers to integrate motion graphics seamlessly into our web projects.

Explore and implement advanced Motion.page features such as image sequences, ScrollTrigger animations, and interactive page transitions.

Stay updated with Motion.page updates and incorporate new features and best practices into projects.



Qualifications:



Proven experience with Motion.page and a portfolio showcasing innovative WordPress sites enhanced with Motion.page animations.

Strong understanding of WordPress, including themes and plugins compatibility.

Creative thinking with attention to detail and a passion for creating interactive web experiences.

Excellent communication and collaboration skills, capable of working in a fast-paced remote environment.



What We Offer:



Remote work flexibility with a competitive salary and benefits package.

A vibrant, innovative team committed to pushing the boundaries of digital marketing in the financial services industry.

Opportunities for professional development and to work on projects that have a real impact.

How to Apply:



Ready to animate our world with Motion.page? Submit your CV and a link to your Motion.page-enhanced portfolio. Show us how you've transformed WordPress sites into dynamic, interactive experiences.

title: Static ad desginer
description:
Hi,



I’m looking for a talented and creative designer to help create high-converting, aesthetic static ads for my e-commerce brand in the baby/mom space.



I’m looking for someone who understands both visual identity and performance marketing.



What I Offer:

- Start date: ASAP

- 100% remote

- Part-time to start (with potential to grow into more hours)

- Long-term opportunity for the right person



What You’ll Be Doing:

- Creating high-end static ads (for Meta, etc.)

- Working with existing content (UGC, product images, lifestyle shots)

- Designing creatives that feel: Clean, Premium, Aesthetic, On-brand

- Testing different angles, hooks and layouts based on performance

- Using modern tools / AI to elevate creatives



Who are you:

- Strong sense of aesthetics & modern design trends

- Experience with e-commerce ads / paid social creatives

- Ability to balance branding + performance

- Skilled in tools like Photoshop, Figma, Canva or similar

- Bonus if you use AI tools to enhance workflow/output



How to Apply:

- A short intro about you

- Examples of static ads you’ve made (especially for e-commerce or paid ads)

- Example of static ads made with purely AI

- Examples where your creatives performed well



Looking forward to hearing from you.



Best,

Olivia

title: Senior Amazon & Walmart Account Manager / Team Lead (Leadership Role – Paid Trial)
description:
Overview:

We are a U.S.-based Amazon & Walmart marketing agency looking for a Senior Account Manager / Team Lead to take ownership of client accounts, manage a remote team, and ensure all work is executed at a high level.



This is a leadership role first, execution second.



You will be responsible for:

-- Keeping accounts running smoothly

-- Ensuring tasks are completed on time

-- Managing tea
mbers

-- Communicating proactively

-- Solving problems before they escalate



We are hiring for this role at a senior level, but all candidates will go through a paid trial period to determine final placement within our internal tier structure based on real performance.



Core Responsibilities:

-- Own and manage multiple Amazon & Walmart client accounts

-- Lead and manage Account Specialists (task delegation, QA, follow-ups)

-- Ensure all daily, weekly, and monthly deliverables are completed on time

-- Monitor and optimize performance (SEO, PPC, TACOS, rankings)

-- Identify issues early and take ownership of solutions

-- Maintain full visibility across all accounts and tasks

-- Ensure proper communication across team and leadership

-- Manage workflows and task tracking in ClickUp

-- Step in directly when tasks are missed or at risk



Platform Requirements (Amazon + Walmart):



Amazon (Required)

-- Seller Central experience (2–4+ years)

-- SEO (keyword indexing, ranking, content optimization)

-- PPC (campaign structure, bidding, performance analysis)



Walmart (Required)

-- Experience with Walmart Seller Center

-- Listing setup and troubleshooting

-- Inventory and pricing management

-- Familiarity with WFS (Walmart Fulfillment Services)

-- Ability to identify and solve issues without relying on support



Non-Negotiable Requirements:

-- Proven experience leading or managing a team

-- Available 30–40 hours per week minimum

-- Daily overlap with PST business hours

-- Strong communication (same-day responses required)

-- High level of accountability and ownership

-- Ability to manage multiple accounts without supervision



Not a Fit If You:

-- Miss deadlines or fail to communicate proactively

-- Prefer task-based work instead of ownership

-- Need constant direction

-- Struggle with time management or availability

-- Are working multiple full-time jobs

-- Avoid responsibility or wait for instructions



How We Work:

-- We prioritize accountability, communication, and ownership

-- We do not micromanage — leaders manage themselves

-- Problems are expected to be solved, not escalated without effort

-- We operate fast and expect high standards

-- Our team supports real businesses — execution matters



Paid Trial (Important):

All candidates start with a paid trial (2–4 weeks).



During this period, you will:

-- Work on real client accounts

-- Be evaluated on communication, ownership, leadership, and execution



Final role placement (Team Lead, Senior Specialist, or lower tier) will be determined based on performance, not your resume.



What Success Looks Like:

-- Tasks are completed on time without follow-ups

-- Communication is proactive and consistent

-- Tea
mbers are aligned and accountable

-- Client issues are identified and resolved early

-- You operate as an owner, not a task executor



Compensation:

-- Based on experience and trial performance

-- Final rate aligned with internal tier structure

-- Growth based on consistency and leadership



Application Instructions:



To apply:



Send your resume

Include the phrase: I take ownership at the top

Answer the following:

-- What would you do if a client’s listing was down for 3 days and no one noticed?

-- How do you handle a tea
mber who is not responding?

-- How do you troubleshoot a suppressed Walmart listing without relying on support?

title: Technical Recruiter - Manufacturing, Construction, and Engineering
description:
We’re Hiring at BOUNDLESS COLLECTIVE!



Who We Are

Boundless Collective is a dynamic, experienced outsourcing powerhouse specializing in delivering creative, admin-backed solutions across industries. We’re not just assistants, we’re innovators, growth enablers, and trusted partners in our clients’ success.



Why You’ll Love It Here

- A high-energy team culture that blends professionalism with camaraderie, where collaboration and creativity are daily drivers.

- Fun-focused atmosphere: from team outings and social events to celebrating milestones big and small, work here feels like more than just work.

- Meaningful recognition: enjoy structured bonuses and reward programs that celebrate your contributions and let you know they’re appreciated.

- Growth-first mindset: as part of our team, you’ll innovate, share ideas, and help shape the future of client solutions every single day.



We’re expanding, and we’re looking for talented individuals to be part of our dynamic team! If you're looking for a fulfilling, impactful role, read on to see how you can contribute and grow with us!



We are hiring for the following positions:



Technical Recruiter - Manufacturing, Construction, and Engineering



What We Offer:

Position: Full-time

Work Setup: Permanent remote – work from anywhere in the world!

Shift: 7AM - 4PM (MNL time) – Start your day with energy!

Equipment: Self-provided – Bring your tech, and we’ll bring the opportunity!

Salary: PHP 40,000 - PHP 50,000 depending on experience. + bonuses



Apply via this link: 
/
KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Manage and support the end-to-end recruitment process, including reviewing client briefs, defining role requirements, writing and posting job ads, sourcing candidates via LinkedIn and applications, and conducting initial screening calls. This role also involves coordinating interviews, maintaining clear candidate communication with stakeholders, organising resumes and documentation, preparing candidate reports and reference checks, and ensuring CRM records, project files, and pipeline updates remain accurate and up to date. A strong ability to map the talent market for targeted skill sets and experience levels is essential.



QUALIFICATIONS

- 3–5+ years recruitment experience, specifically within engineering, manufacturing, or construction roles.

- Advanced proficiency in LinkedIn Recruiter (essential) with demonstrated experience in talent mapping and outreach.

- Strong ability to engage and communicate with highly qualified, English-speaking candidates at a professional level.

- Demonstrated ability to understand technical job requirements.

- Recent experience recruiting for similar roles and industries aligned with the client’s core focus (as advertised on Seek).

- Experience using Seek Talent Search and ATS/CRM platforms such as JobAdder (preferred).

- Daily use of AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT or similar) to support productivity, sourcing, or workflow optimisation.

- Demonstrated ability to run end-to-end recruitment, not only sourcing.

- Ability to quickly understand role requirements and assess candidate fit.

- Exceptional English communication skills (written and spoken), critical due to direct candidate calling.

- Confident phone manner and ability to conduct structured screening interviews.

- Strong reporting and documentation skills (candidate write-ups and reference checks).

- Highly organised, process-driven, and able to manage multiple roles.



At Boundless Collective, we take pride in fostering a culture of confidence, reliability, and a relentless commitment to excellence. If you are ready to support a dynamic executive team and grow in a supportive environment, we’d love to hear from you.



Ready to make an impact and elevate your career? Apply via link below and let's create something extraordinary together!



APPLY HERE: 
/

title: Outbound Sales Specialist
description:
We’re a growing commercial cleaning company based in Melbourne, Australia.



We’re looking for an experienced Outbound Sales Specialist to build lists of target companies and set up outbound sales campaigns.



RESPONSIBILITIES:

- Research and identify high-quality target companies based on defined criteria

- Build and maintain clean, well-structured lead lists

- Set up and manage cold
 campaigns

- Write and refine
 messaging to improve response rates

- Continuously improve targeting and campaigns based on results



REQUIREMENTS:

- Experience setting up and managing B2B cold
 campaigns

- Strong written English (clear, natural, non-spammy tone)

- Ability to research companies and identify good-fit prospects

title: Senior Estimator W/ Project Manager Experience
description:
ABOUT US



JLC and Schear LLC are New York and New Jersey based interior construction companies operating as both specialty subcontractors and general contractors. We self-perform all of our trades across six divisions — high-rise production, retail and corporate fit-out, retail refresh, public and government, interior GC, and maintenance and repair. Our primary markets are New York City and New Jersey. Our secondary markets are Pennsylvania, Delaware, Maryland, Virginia, Washington D.C., Florida, and Connecticut.



We are growing fast and we are looking for one exceptional Senior Estimator and Project Manager to join our team. This is not an entry level role and it is not for someone who is learning on the job. We need someone who already knows how to build.



WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR



We are looking for a Senior Estimator and Project Manager who has deep knowledge of U.S. commercial interior construction — specifically the Northeast United States market. You must understand how buildings are built, how long things take, what materials cost, and how to put together a competitive number that wins work and makes money.



You will be estimating projects ranging from $75,000 to $15,000,000 across multiple project types — retail fit-outs, corporate offices, medical facilities, high-rise residential, public buildings, government facilities, and more.



This role requires full ownership. You are not assisting anyone. You are the estimator. You own the number from takeoff to submission and you own the project from award to closeout.



WHAT THIS ROLE REQUIRES



You must have real experience with all of the following. If you do not have this experience do not apply.



- We are not looking for just quantitative takeoffs, We want a real estimator with USA experience and knows how to build and how long it takes to build.

- Quantitative takeoffs for metal framing, drywall, ACT ceilings, flooring, painting, and general finishes

- Scope writing from drawings — independently — without being told what to include

- Competitive bid preparation for commercial interior projects in the U.S. Northeast market

- Full project management lifecycle — RFIs, submittals, change orders, scheduling, purchasing, ACR tracking

- AIA billing — Schedule of Values, requisitions, and pencil requisition review

- Subcontractor RFP solicitation, bid leveling, and award

- Davis-Bacon wage rates and prevailing wage compliance — strong knowledge preferred

- Public and federal work bidding — 
, state procurement portals — preferred

- Procore, Bluebeam, Estimator Edge, and advanced Excel — required

- Revit — preferred



WHAT WE DO NOT WANT



We will screen for this directly in our questionnaire. Do not apply if any of the following describes you:



- You use AI to generate takeoffs or estimates

- You have only supported estimators — you have never owned the number yourself

- You do not know Northeast U.S. labor rates, material costs, or market conditions

- You cannot complete a full estimate on a $75K–$250K interior project in 3 to 3.5 hours

- You cannot explain construction sequencing, trade coordination, or scheduling logic without looking it up



WHAT YOU WILL DO



- Complete full quantitative takeoffs and estimates independently

- Write scopes of work from drawings without prompting

- Submit all estimates to the executive team 2 days before actual bid due date

- Own RFI log, submittal log, change order log, and project schedule simultaneously on active projects

- Handle purchasing end to end — solicit quotes, level bids, issue purchase orders

- Write and submit AIA requisitions and Schedules of Values

- Manage subcontractor relationships and performance

- Respond to executive team within 15 minutes, GC/client within 30 minutes, urgent WhatsApp within 10 minutes

- Assist fellow estimator across all divisions as needed



DETAILS



- Hours: 10:00AM to 6:00PM EST — Monday through Friday

- Rate: $8.00 to $10.00 per hour based on demonstrated ability

- Location: Remote — Philippines based

- Start: Immediate



HOW TO APPLY



Read every question below carefully. Answer every single question. Be specific. Use real numbers, real project names, real outcomes. We are not looking for general answers. We are looking for proof that you have actually done this work.



The filter phrase for this role is: I know how to build.



Put this phrase at the very top of your response before you answer a single question. If it is not there your application will not be read.



SCREENING QUESTIONNAIRE



Answer every question. Be specific. Vague answers will be disqualified immediately.



SECTION 1 — CONSTRUCTION KNOWLEDGE — U.S. NORTHEAST MARKET



These questions have specific correct answers. If you do not know them you do not have the experience we need.



1. What is the current labor rate range for a journeyman drywall finisher in New York City? Give a specific number range per hour.



2. What is the difference between Level 4 and Level 5 drywall finish? When would you specify each one and why?



3. In New York City commercial construction what is the standard metal stud spacing for a non-load-bearing partition wall? What gauge stud would you use for a 14-foot wall height?



4. What is the difference between 5/8 inch Type X gypsum board and 5/8 inch Type C gypsum board? When is each one required?



5. A GC asks you to include a price for a 2-hour fire-rated wall assembly in a high-rise building. Walk me through exactly how you would build that assembly and what materials you would include in your takeoff.



6. What is Kaydex and where is it typically used in commercial interior construction?



7. What is the typical cost per square foot range to frame and drywall a standard commercial office partition in the New York and New Jersey market — both labor and material combined? Give a specific number range.



8. What is the difference between ACT grid and drywall ceiling systems in terms of cost, installation time, and application? When would you recommend each one?



9. A project in Manhattan requires exterior metal framing with Densglass sheathing. What are the key installation requirements and what are the most common field errors you need to watch for?



10. What is FRP and where is it used? Give three specific applications in commercial interior construction.



SECTION 2 — ESTIMATING ABILITY



11. Walk me through your complete estimating process from the 
nt you receive a set of drawings to the 
nt you submit a number. Be specific about every step.



12. What estimating software do you use for takeoffs? How long have you used it and what is your proficiency level — beginner, intermediate, or expert? Give a specific example of a complex takeoff you completed with it.



13. A retail fit-out in New Jersey is 8,500 square feet. Scope includes metal stud framing, drywall, ACT ceilings, painting, and LVT flooring. How long would it take you to complete a full quantitative takeoff and estimate? Walk me through your time breakdown.



14. Describe a time your estimate came in significantly higher than the awarded price. What happened? What did you learn? What would you do differently?



15. How do you handle scope gaps in a set of drawings? Give a specific example of a scope gap you identified that others missed and how you addressed it.



16. What is your process for writing a scope of work independently from a set of drawings? Give me an example of a scope you wrote for a project over $500,000.



17. How do you verify material quantities in your takeoff? What is your quality control process before you submit a number?



18. Have you ever estimated a project using Davis-Bacon wage rates? If yes describe the project — type, size, location, and dollar value. Explain how Davis-Bacon rates affected your number compared to open shop rates.



19. Have you ever bid a public or government project — federal, state, or municipal? If yes describe the project and the procurement process you went through.



20. Are you familiar with 
, NJSTART, or the NYS Contract Reporter? Have you ever used any of these platforms to find or submit bids? Describe your experience.



SECTION 3 — PROJECT MANAGEMENT OWNERSHIP



21. Have you personally owned an RFI log from open to close — not assisted, not supported, owned? Describe a specific project where you managed the full RFI process. How many RFIs were on the log? What was the most complex one and how did you resolve it?



22. Have you owned a full submittal log from initiation through approval? Describe the process you used. What happens when a submittal gets rejected?



23. Have you managed change orders independently — written them, negotiated them, and closed them? Describe a specific change order dispute you resolved. What was the dollar value and what was the outcome?



24. Have you built and maintained a project schedule independently? What software did you use? Describe a project where the schedule was severely impacted and how you managed it.



25. Describe a project where you were managing RFIs, submittals, change orders, purchasing, and scheduling simultaneously. How did you keep everything moving without dropping anything?



26. Have you handled purchasing end to end — solicited quotes, leveled bids, issued purchase orders, and tracked deliveries? Describe your purchasing process on a project over $200,000.



27. Have you written AIA G702 and G703 requisitions independently? Describe your process for building a Schedule of Values at the start of a project.



28. Have you managed subcontractor performance on a project? Describe a situation where a subcontractor was underperforming and how you handled it.



SECTION 4 — TOOLS AND SOFTWARE



29. Rate your proficiency in each of the following on a scale of 1 to 10 and describe specifically how you use each one:

- Bluebeam Revu

- Estimator Edge

- Procore

- Microsoft Excel

- Revit



30. Do you use AI tools to assist with takeoffs or estimates? Be honest. If yes describe exactly how you use them and what you use them for.



31. Have you ever used Procore for RFI management, submittal tracking, and change order logs simultaneously on a live project? Describe your workflow.



SECTION 5 — AVAILABILITY AND SETUP



32. Confirm your available hours in EST — specifically 10:00AM to 6:00PM Monday through Friday. Are there any conflicts?



33. Describe your computer setup — processor, RAM, monitor setup, and internet speed. This role requires running Bluebeam, Estimator Edge, Procore, and Excel simultaneously.



34. Do you have a quiet dedicated workspace with no background noise during work hours?



35. Can you provide three work samples a full quantitative takeoff, a scope of work you wrote independently, and a project schedule you built? Names and confidential information can be redacted. These are required before any interview will be scheduled.



Remember put "I know how to build" at the very top of your response or your application will not be read.



JLC / Schear — New York and New Jersey — April 2026

title: Marketplace SEO Manager – Amazon & Walmart (Agency Experience Required)
description:
IMPORTANT – PLEASE READ BEFORE APPLYING



This role is ONLY for candidates who have worked at an Amazon or Walmart AGENCY managing multiple brands simultaneously.



If your experience is limited to:

-- A single brand

-- VA/task-based execution

-- Following SOPs without owning outcomes



Please do NOT apply.



ROLE OVERVIEW

We are hiring for a Senior SEO Manager / Leadership Track role responsible for Amazon and Walmart listing optimization across multiple client accounts.



This is a high-level ownership role, not a junior execution role.



You will be responsible for:

-- Setting strategy

-- Approving and rejecting work

-- Mentoring tea
mbers

-- Driving measurable improvements in ranking, indexation, conversion, and revenue



Final role level (Senior, Lead, or Manager) will be determined through a paid trial project based on actual performance, not just experience.



CORE RESPONSIBILITIES

--- Own SEO performance across multiple client accounts (ranking, traffic, conversion)

--- Lead keyword research and clustering strategy for new and existing listings

--- Review, refine, approve, or reject listing copy before publication

--- Ensure correct keyword placement across all listing elements

--- Diagnose and resolve indexation, ranking, and conversion issues

--- Align SEO with PPC and Design teams for full-funnel performance

--- Train, mentor, and review SEO specialists and listing tea
mbers

--- Build and improve SEO SOPs for scalability

--- Monitor performance and proactively identify optimization opportunities

--- Provide clear, strategic recommendations to leadership



REQUIRED EXPERIENCE (NON-NEGOTIABLE)

--- 3+ years at an Amazon or Walmart AGENCY (multi-brand experience required)

--- Experience managing SEO across multiple brands simultaneously

--- Experience reviewing and approving/rejecting work from other tea
mbers

--- Strong understanding of keyword intent, SERP behavior, and conversion strategy

--- Proven ability to diagnose and fix underperforming listings

--- Ability to clearly explain strategy and decision-making



TOOLS EXPERIENCE REQUIRED



You must have experience using these tools, but must NOT rely on them blindly.

--- DataDive and similar clustering tools

--- Helium 10 (Cerebro, Magnet, Listing Analyzer, Index Checker)

--- Amazon Brand Analytics

--- Amazon Search Term Reports

--- Walmart Seller Center tools

--- Advanced Google Sheets



WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

--- Leadership mindset (not just execution)

--- Strong independent thinking and judgment

--- Ability to make decisions without perfect data

--- Process builder and tea
ntor

--- High accountability and ownership

--- Ability to operate across multiple accounts without losing quality



COMPENSATION & STRUCTURE

--- All candidates must complete a paid trial project (2 weeks)

--- Trial performance determines final role placement:

     -- Senior SEO Specialist

     -- SEO Team Lead

     -- SEO Manager

--- Compensation will be aligned with proven performance during the trial

--- Long-term opportunity with growth into leadership



APPLICATION REQUIREMENTS



A resume is REQUIRED. Applications without a resume will not be reviewed.



You must answer ALL of the following:

--- Which Amazon or Walmart agencies have you worked for?

--- How many brands did you manage at one time?

--- What was your role in approving or rejecting listing changes?

--- Which SEO tools do you use, and when do you ignore them?

--- How important is ChatGPT or AI tools in your workflow?

--- How often should SEO/indexing be performed on a listing?

--- How do you manage multiple brands and maintain SEO cadence?

--- How would you structure an SEO team (roles, responsibilities, capacity)?



???? Final Question (Required):



Walk through your exact process for taking a non-indexed keyword and getting it ranked on page 1.



Be specific. General answers will not be considered.

mbers to improve outreach strategies and performance

We are an established U.S.–based healthcare services provider seeking a skilled and highly adaptable software engineer to support and improve an existing internal workflow platform. Our focus is on increasing operational efficiency, automating processes, and streamlining daily workflows across multiple care settings.



 Role Overview



We are looking for a developer who can quickly understand an existing system, resolve issues, and implement new features with minimal ramp-up time. This is a hands-on, project-based role for someone who can start immediately and contribute right away.



You will work directly with leadership to translate real-world operational needs into functional software improvements.

, APIs)

 Preferred Experience

* Background in low-code/no-code platforms or similar systems

* Experience with API integrations and system connectivity

* Understanding of user permissions and structured data systems

* Strong problem-solving and communication skills

* Medical or healthcare software experience is a plus





 What We’re Looking For



* Ability to start immediately

* Fast learner who can pick up existing systems quickly

 What We Offer

 How to Apply

title: BD Pipeline Manager – Construction
description:
ABOUT US



JLC and Schear LLC are New York and New Jersey based interior construction companies operating as both specialty subcontractors and general contractors. We self-perform all of our trades across six divisions. Our primary markets are New York City and New Jersey. Our secondary markets are Pennsylvania, Delaware, Maryland, Virginia, Washington D.C., Florida, and Connecticut.



We are looking for a BD Operations Lead who understands U.S. commercial construction, knows how to research with real depth, and can execute a defined system with precision and accountability. This is not a data entry role. This is not a research assistant role. This is a high responsibility operations position where the quality of your work directly drives the revenue of this company.



THE ROLE — EXACTLY WHAT YOU WILL DO



You are the single point of intake for every business development lead that enters this company. Every lead that comes in — from PlanHub, from 
, from direct
s, from any source — comes to you first. You are the gatekeeper and the operator. You are also to assist the callers Nick and Mike and
 and do research for them. Nick and Mike are the ones who call and make contact they must remain on the phone making calls all day, its your job to assist them!



Here is the exact daily workflow you will execute without exception:



LEAD INTAKE:

When a lead arrives you immediately identify the correct division it belongs to. Division 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 Identifying the correct division requires real understanding of commercial construction — you must know the difference between high-rise production, retail fit-out, refresh work, public work, interior GC projects, and maintenance work. Getting this wrong is not acceptable.



BID BOARD LOGGING:

Every lead gets logged on the master bid board the 
nt it comes in. Company name, contact, phone,
, project name, location, division, deadline, project type, tier, and all relevant notes. No missed fields. No wrong divisions. No typos. The bid board is the source of truth for this company.



GC IDENTIFICATION:

For every project on PlanHub you pull all GCs listed as bidding the project. You add every single one of them to Nick and Mike's call sheets so they can reach out. Missing a GC on a project is a missed opportunity and those are not acceptable.



CALENDAR:

Every confirmed bid gets logged on the master calendar 2 days before the actual external due date. This buffer is non-negotiable. The estimator must have time to review.



LNB DRIVE:

Every confirmed bid gets a project folder on the LNB Drive. All plans, specs, addenda, and bid documents are downloaded and organized properly. The estimator accesses everything from there.



CALL SHEETS:

You build the daily call sheets for Nick and Mike. They are delivered by 5:00PM every day — ready for the following morning. Every call sheet entry includes company, contact, direct phone, project reference, and background. Nick and Mike never call cold.



CALL LOGGING:

When Nick and Mike report back on their calls you log their notes accurately and in detail on the bid tracker. Every call outcome. Every status update. Every next step. This is what Abi and Lyka have been failing to do and it is unacceptable.



RESEARCH:

You continuously research old contacts, cold leads, and new opportunities. You find decision makers. You identify new angles. You build the pipeline.



-DIVISIONS YOU WILL SUPPORT



You must understand all six divisions well enough to correctly identify and route every lead.



Division 1 — High-Rise Production: Residential towers, mixed-use, hotels, high-rise office buildings.



Division 2 — Retail & Corporate Fit-Out: DIV 9 WORK Interior build-outs, tenant improvements, retail stores, corporate offices, medical offices.



Division 3 — Retail & Corporate Refresh: Store refreshes, rebranding programs, office refresh, fast cycle renovations. Target brands include CVS, Chase, Wells Fargo, Aspen Dental, Ross, Burlington, TJ Maxx. Property management companies — JLL, Newmark, CBRE, Cushman & Wakefield, Greystar, Avalon Bay, First Service Residential.



Division 4 — Public & Government: Schools, municipal buildings, government offices, universities, public hospitals, transit facilities. Requires company registration on 
, NJSTART, NY Cost Reporter, NYS Contract Reporter, all 21 NJ counties, Port Authority, MTA, CUNY, SUNY, NYC Health + Hospitals.



Division 5 — Interior GC: Full interior general contracting. Targets are developers, owners reps, property managers, architects, universities, hospitals. Related Companies, Brookfield, Tishman Speyer, Silverstein, Hill International, AECOM, Columbia University, NYU, NYU Langone, Mount Sinai.



Division 6 — Maintenance & Repair: Ongoing maintenance contracts, tenant turnovers, punch list work. Key platforms are ServiceChannel and Corrigo. Target property managers and facility management companies.



PLATFORMS YOU WILL USE DAILY



- PlanHub — primary platform for leads

- 
 — federal opportunities — check every morning

- NYS Contract Reporter

- NJSTART

- NY Cost Reporter

- BidNet Direct

- DemandStar

- ServiceChannel and Corrigo — Division 6

- LinkedIn — direct outreach and research

- CoStar, Dodge Data, BuildingConnected, ConstructConnect — as we scale



DETAILS



- Hours: 9:00AM to 5:00PM EST — Monday through Friday

- Rate: $8.00 to $10.00 per hour based on demonstrated ability and experience

- Location: Remote — Philippines based

- Start: Immediate



WHAT WILL DISQUALIFY YOU IMMEDIATELY



- You use AI to generate research or responses

- You cannot name major U.S. commercial construction project types and identify which division they belong to

- You cannot describe a complete BD workflow for a construction company without looking it up

- You have never worked with PlanHub, 
, or any U.S. construction platform

- Your answers to the questionnaire below are vague, generic, or obviously AI generated



HOW TO APPLY



Read every question below carefully. Answer every single question. Be specific. Use real examples from your actual work history. Generic answers will be disqualified.



The filter phrase for this role is: I own the pipeline.



Put this phrase at the very top of your response before you answer a single question. If it is not there your application will not be read.



SCREENING QUESTIONNAIRE — 40 QUESTIONS



SECTION 1 — U.S. CONSTRUCTION KNOWLEDGE



These have specific correct answers. If you do not know them you do not have the experience we need.



1. What is the difference between a retail fit-out and a retail refresh? Give a specific example of each.



2. What is a white box build-out? Who pays for it — the landlord or the tenant?



3. What is the difference between a high-rise project and a commercial fit-out project? How would you identify each one from a PlanHub lead?



4. A PlanHub lead shows a project at a Chase Bank branch in New Jersey — scope is new flooring, new paint, ceiling replacement, and new signage. Which of our six divisions does this belong to?



5. A project is listed as a 40,000 square foot ground-up construction of a public middle school in Essex County New Jersey. Which division is this? What must we have completed before we can bid it?



6. What is the difference between Division 9 work and general contracting? Why does it matter which one we are bidding as?



7. What is prevailing wage and where does it apply? Name two U.S. platforms where prevailing wage rates are published.



8. What is a bid bond? What is a performance bond? When is each one required?



9. What are the major general contractors that dominate public work in New York and New Jersey? Name five.



10. What are the major property management companies operating in NYC and NJ that we would target for refresh and maintenance work? Name five.



SECTION 2 — PLATFORM KNOWLEDGE



11. Have you used PlanHub before? If yes describe exactly what you used it for — be specific about features and daily workflow.



12. Have you used 
? What is it used for? What information is required to register a company on it?



13. What is NJSTART? What is the NYS Contract Reporter? What is the NY Cost Reporter? Describe the purpose of each one.



14. What is ServiceChannel? What is Corrigo? Who uses these platforms and what work is dispatched through them?



15. What is CoStar and how would it be useful for identifying commercial construction opportunities?



16. What is BuildingConnected and how is it different from PlanHub?



SECTION 3 — RESEARCH AND DEEP DIVE ABILITY



17. A PlanHub lead comes through for a 15,000 sf retail build-out in Newark NJ. You have the project name and the address. Walk me through every step you would take over the next 30 minutes to research this lead fully and add it to the bid board.



18. How do you identify the decision maker at a property management company? Walk me through your process.



19. What is the difference between surface level research and deep research in your own words? Give an example of each.



20. How do you find and verify a direct phone number and direct
 for a decision maker? Do not tell me you use Google — tell me the specific tools and techniques you use.



21. Have you ever used 
, ZoomInfo, RocketReach, or 
? Which ones and how did you use them?



22. A cold lead has gone silent for three months. Walk me through exactly what you would do to reignite that relationship with new intelligence.



SECTION 4 — BD WORKFLOW OWNERSHIP



23. Describe a BD workflow you have owned from lead intake to bid submission in a previous role. Use specific numbers — how many leads per day, how many active bids at once, how many GCs you tracked.



24. A lead comes in at 3:00PM on a Friday. Bid is due Monday at 12:00PM. What is the order of every single action you take over the next 72 hours?



25. You have 30 active leads on a bid board. Three of them are HOT, ten are WARM, seventeen are COLD. Walk me through how you prioritize your time across all 30 in a single day.



26. You notice Nick called a GC yesterday but did not report back. How do you handle it?



27. Mike reports that a GC he called today has an awarded project that needs subs by next Thursday. What are the first five things you do in the next 15 minutes?



28. A bid is due tomorrow and the estimator has not received the plans because you forgot to save them to the LNB Drive. What do you do and how do you ensure this never happens again?



SECTION 5 — ATTENTION TO DETAIL



29. A PlanHub lead shows the following information — Project Name: Starbucks Refresh — Location: 123 Main St Bridgewater NJ 08807 — Due Date: April 15 — Scope: Flooring, paint, fixtures — GCs bidding: ABC Builders, XYZ Construction, Delta Contractors. Tell me exactly which division this belongs to, what internal deadline you would set, and what you would enter in the GC list field on the bid board.



30. What are the three most common mistakes a BD Operations person can make when logging a lead on the bid board? How would you prevent each one?



31. A lead comes in with the project address listed as Bergen County but the actual project is in Union County. How do you verify the correct location and what do you do with the information?



32. Describe your personal quality control process before you submit anything for review.



SECTION 6 — AI USE AND HONESTY



33. Do you use AI tools — ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, Grok, or any others — in your work? Be completely honest. If yes describe exactly what you use them for.



34. If you are hired and we require zero AI use in your daily output — specifically in research, lead identification, and call sheet building — is that acceptable to you? Explain why or why not.



35. We will test your knowledge with follow-up verbal or video calls. How comfortable are you defending your written answers in a live conversation?



SECTION 7 — AVAILABILITY AND SETUP



36. Confirm your available hours are 9:00AM to 5:00PM EST — Monday through Friday. Any conflicts?



37. Describe your computer setup — processor, RAM, monitor setup, and internet speed. This role requires running PlanHub, 
, LinkedIn, Excel or Google Sheets, Gmail, and WhatsApp simultaneously throughout the day.



38. Do you have a quiet dedicated workspace with no background noise during work hours?



39. What is your expected hourly rate? Be honest — if you say $10 and your experience supports it we will pay it.



40. Can you provide two work samples — a research deliverable you produced and a tracker or bid board you built or maintained? Names and confidential information can be redacted. These are required before any interview will be scheduled.



Final reminder — put "I own the pipeline" at the very top of your response or your application will not be read. Every question must be answered. Vague answers will be disqualified.



JLC / Schear — New York and New Jersey — April 2026

title: Google Ads Specialist
description:
Apply here: 



About the Company

Join a leading UK performance-marketing agency running two fast-growing brands—one focused on scalable paid media for e-commerce and service businesses, and the other specializing in local lead generation for home-improvement and trade clients. With over 10 years of trusted client partnerships and bold growth plans to double revenue within 24 months, this agency is built on ownership, reliability, and proactive problem-solving.



Key Responsibilities:

- Manage, optimise, and scale multiple Google Ads accounts for e-commerce and lead-gen clients.

- Maintain strong performance against CPA, ROAS, and conversion targets.

- Audit accounts regularly to identify new growth opportunities.

- Lead client calls, presenting actionable insights and clear performance updates.

- Implement effective funnel strategies to improve lead quality and conversion efficiency.

- Set up, monitor, and troubleshoot GA4 tracking and GTM tags to ensure accurate attribution.

- Collaborate with the internal creative and strategy teams to enhance campaign performance.

- Proactively recommend process improvements and contribute to the agency’s growth strategy.



Requirements

- 3–5+ years of hands-on Google Ads management (agency experience strongly preferred).

- Proven expertise in lead-generation and e-commerce campaigns.

- Proficiency in GA4 and Google Tag Manager (GTM), including event tracking and conversion setup.

- Strong data interpretation and analytical reporting skills.

- Excellent spoken and written English; confident presenting to clients.

- Ability to juggle multiple accounts independently and maintain consistent quality.

- Reliable internet connection and availability for UK business-hour overlap (4–5 hours daily).

- Personality fit: proactive, detail-oriented, transparent, and driven to improve systems and processes.

title: Paid Media Specialist (Google & Meta Ads)
description:
We are seeking a highly analytical and performance-oriented Performance Marketing Specialist to manage, optimize, and scale paid advertising campaigns across Google Ads and Meta 
 & Instagram) platforms.

This role plays a key part in driving qualified leads, customer acquisition, and revenue growth through strategic planning, hands-on execution, and ongoing performance optimization.



I. Key Responsibilities

Plan, execute, and manage paid media campaigns across Google Ads (Search, Display, YouTube, Performance Max) and Meta Ads platforms.

Conduct audience research, keyword analysis, and competitor benchmarking to improve campaign performance.

Monitor, analyze, and optimize campaigns to achieve KPIs such as CPA, ROAS, CTR, CPC, and conversion rate.

Collaborate with creative and content teams to develop and test high-performing ad creatives and messaging.

Allocate and manage advertising budgets effectively to maximize ROI and scale successful campaigns.

Implement and maintain accurate tracking systems using Google Analytics (GA4), Google Tag Manager, Meta Pixel, and other tracking tools.

Prepare weekly and monthly performance reports with actionable insights and optimization recommendations.

Stay updated on industry trends, platform updates, and emerging performance marketing best practices.

Work cross-functionally to align paid media strategies with overall marketing and business objectives.



II. Qualifications & Requirements

1–3+ years of hands-on experience in Paid Media or Digital Advertising.

Proven experience managing campaigns on Google Ads and Meta Ads platforms.

Strong analytical skills with a data-driven decision-making approach.

Experience with performance tracking, analytics, and reporting tools.

Ability to manage multiple campaigns simultaneously and meet deadlines.

Excellent communication and teamwork skills.



III. Preferred Qualifications

Experience in lead generation and/or e-commerce performance campaigns.

Knowledge of A/B testing, remarketing strategies, and funnel optimization.

Experience managing medium to large advertising budgets.



IV. What We Offer

Competitive salary with performance-based incentives.

Opportunity to work with diverse industries and fast-growing brands.

Dynamic and fast-paced performance marketing environment.

Continuous learning and professional development opportunities.

Clear career progression pathways.

Please submit your CV to:

title: Affiliate Manager - TikTok Shop
description:
Reformly is a fast-growing home Pilates brand helping women aged 35–65 feel stronger, healthier, and more confident from home.



Our mission is simple: give women everything they need to feel strong, confident, and unstoppable.



We’re a tight-knit team of 12 full-time people who move fast, care deeply about quality, and genuinely love what we build. Every person who joins Reformly gets the opportunity to grow with us long-term. We’re not hiring for a quick project or an agency. We’re looking for someone who wants to be part of something bigger and help shape our culture as we scale.



The Role



We’re looking for a full-time Affiliate Manager who will take ownership of growing and managing our affiliate program, with a strong focus on TikTok Shop and creator partnerships at scale.



This is not a basic outreach role. This is not a “send a few messages and hope for the best” role.



You’ll be the person responsible for building strong relationships with affiliates and creators, identifying growth opportunities, managing ongoing performance, and helping Reformly scale through high-quality partnerships that actually drive results.



You should already have experience with TikTok Shop and know what it takes to recruit, manage, and grow affiliates at scale. You understand how to spot potential, how to keep creators engaged, and how to turn affiliate relationships into a serious growth channel.



Alongside affiliate management, you’ll also oversee our social media pages and make sure our channels stay active, on-brand, and aligned with the wider marketing strategy.



You’ll work closely with the team to execute campaigns, coordinate creators, track performance, and keep everything moving fast and efficiently. If you also have experience with Figma, that’s a strong plus.



What You’ll Own



• Affiliate recruitment: finding, reaching out to, and onboarding new affiliates and creators 

• TikTok Shop affiliate growth: building and scaling our affiliate presence on TikTok Shop 

• Affiliate relationship management: maintaining strong communication and long-term partnerships with creators 

• Performance management: tracking affiliate results, identifying top performers, and improving overall output 

• Campaign coordination: making sure creators have the right products, briefs, timelines, and follow-up 

• Social media management: overseeing Reformly’s social media pages and helping maintain a strong brand presence 

• Growth opportunities: spotting new ways to expand reach, improve performance, and scale partnerships faster



What We’re Looking For



Must-have



• Proven experience in affiliate marketing, influencer management, or creator partnerships 

• Strong hands-on experience with TikTok Shop • Experience managing affiliates or creators at scale 

• Strong communication and relationship-building skills 

• Ability to stay organized while managing a high volume of conversations, follow-ups, and partnerships 

• Commercial mindset with a clear understanding of what drives performance and sales 

• Ability to move fast, take ownership, and keep things structured in a scaling environment 

• High attention to detail and strong execution skills



Nice to have



• Experience managing brand social media pages • Understanding of content that performs well on TikTok and other social platforms • Experience in e-commerce, direct response, or consumer brands • Figma skills • A self-driven mindset with a strong focus on growth, performance, and continuous improvement



Why Reformly



• Full-time, long-term opportunity in a company that’s growing fast 

• Real ownership over one of the most important growth channels in the business 

• Direct collaboration with a highly involved team that cares deeply about quality and execution 

• The chance to grow with the company and play a meaningful role in shaping the brand as we scale 

• Your voice matters here, and your growth matters just as much as the company’s



How to Apply



Video applications only.



Please send us:



• A short introduction video telling us about yourself

• Your experience with TikTok Shop and affiliate management

• Examples of results you’ve achieved or partnerships you’ve managed

• Why you believe you’d be a strong fit for Reformly



This is not optional. Applications without a video will not be considered.

title: Affiliate Marketing and Social Media Manager

Recruit new affiliates through
,

, Discord, TikTok, and other platforms

Run giveaways, bonus campaigns, and audience growth strategies

4. SEO & Traffic Growth

Contact websites for guest posts, listings, mentions, and backlinks



Coordinate blog content ideas and traffic campaigns



Help improve organic search presence in target markets



5. User Acquisition Focus

Constantly search for new traffic sources



Test communities, influencers, paid placements, and viral channels



Focus on quality users who deposit and stay active



Bring ideas, not excuses



Requirements

Strong English communication skills



Excellent online research and outreach skills



Comfortable using
, Discord, X, Instagram,
, and Google tools

Big Plus

Crypto experience



SEO experience

Hungry



Fast



Street smart



Professional



Persistent



Results-driven



Creative problem solver



KPIs / Success Metrics

Social media growth and engagement

Weekly traffic growth

Performance bonuses



Long-term growth opportunity with an international brand



Work Setup

Remote



Full-time 



Flexible hours, results-based culture



How to Apply

Resume / background



Relevant experience



Any results you’ve achieved (traffic, affiliates, growth, social media, SEO, etc.)



Why you are a good fit for Trillix



Final Note

We are not looking for someone to “manage tasks.”



We are looking for someone who can help build a real growth engine and bring users consistently.

title: Part-Time Virtual Assistant - Real Estate Outreach & Listings Management
description:
About Us

Casae Living is a Perth-based co-living operator. We lease residential properties from landlords on fixed-term head leases and manage them as shared living homes. We're growing fast and looking for a sharp, phone-confident VA to help us find new properties and fill rooms.

The Role

This is a part-time role, 4 hours per day, Monday to Friday, with the potential to grow into a full-time position for the right person. The work splits roughly across two areas:

1. Property Outreach (primary focus)



Research residential rental listings on 
 and other platforms daily

Filter properties against our criteria (we'll give you a clear checklist)

Call landlords and property managers to introduce Casae Living and our head lease model

Your goal is not to close anyone -- just to explain what we do, answer basic questions, and book a viewing for one of our directors if there's interest

Log every call outcome in a shared tracker (we'll set this up for you)



2. 
 Management (secondary focus)



Post and manage room listings across our properties

Respond to inbound enquiries promptly

Reach out proactively to active renter profiles that match our properties

Coordinate viewing bookings with our team



What We're Looking For



Excellent spoken and written English -- this role involves regular calls with Australian landlords and agents

Comfortable with outbound cold calling -- this is a phone-heavy role and that's a non-negotiable

Organised and self-directed -- you'll be working independently during your shift

Reliable internet connection and a quiet space for calls

Prior experience in outbound calling, appointment setting, or real estate admin is a strong advantage but not required if you can demonstrate the right communication skills



To Apply

Please send your resume along with a short voice or video recording (1-2 minutes) introducing yourself and explaining why you'd be a good fit for this role. Applications without a recording will not be considered.

Details



20 hours per week (4 hours/day, Mon-Fri)

Australian business hours (Perth time, AWST)

$4-8 USD/hour depending on experience

Part-time to start, with potential to grow into a full-time role for the right person

Long-term ongoing position

title: Client Coach (Coaching and Feedback) – $1,100/month
description:
Full-Time | Long-Term Role



Start at $935 per month base.

eting (mandatory, 3pm CET)

title: Real Estate Lead Qualification Specialist — Full Time, PH Daytime Hours, $800–$1,200/mo + Bonus
description:
We are a growing real estate & vacation rental digital platform hiring a full-time Lead Qualification Specialist to respond to inbound leads within 15 minutes, qualify them using our script, and hand warm prospects to our sales team through our Kommo CRM. You'll work standard Philippine daytime hours — no night shift.



This is not a cold calling role. You will be working inbound leads that have already raised their hand. Your job is to respond fast, ask smart qualifying questions, and d
 who gets passed to our closers.

We are looking for someone who wants a long-term home — we invest in our people, pay on time, provide performance bonuses, and promote from within as we scale toward 24/7 lead coverage.



What You'll Do



Monitor new leads in Kommo CRM and reply / call within 15 minutes during your shift

Qualify leads using our provided script (timeline, budget, motivation, location)

Log all conversations and qualification notes clearly in Kommo

Book warm leads directly onto our sales team's calendars

Send follow-up messages to unresponsive leads on a defined cadence

Flag high-priority leads to the sales team immediately via our messaging system

Hit weekly KPIs: response time, qualification rate, handoff acceptance



What We Require (please do not apply if you don't meet these)



Minimum 1 year experience in inside sales, SDR, lead qualification, or a similar role

Excellent written and spoken English — you will be messaging and calling worldwide clients directly

CRM experience (Kommo, HubSpot, GoHighLevel, Salesforce, or similar) — Kommo specifically is a big plus

Reliable home internet (minimum 25 Mbps) and backup power solution

Quiet work environment with a good headset for occasional calls

Availability for 9am–6pm Philippine Time (same as Bali/WITA), Monday–Friday, with occasional Saturday coverage

At least 40 hours/week commitment — this is a full-time role, not a side hustle.



Bonus Points For



Prior real estate industry experience (Bali, SE Asia, or international markets especially)

Experience with Kommo CRM specifically

Experience using WhatsApp Business, SMS, and
 for lead communication

Previous role with strict response SLAs.



Compensation



$800–$1,200 USD/month depending on experience (paid bi-monthly via Wise)

Performance bonus tied to qualified-lead conversion rate



How to Apply — READ CAREFULLY

To show us you read this entire post, your application must:



Start with the phrase: "I'm your real estate lead qualifier"

Answer these four questions in the body of your application:



Q1: Describe one time you had to qualify an inbound lead. What questions did you ask and how did you d
 if they were warm?

Q2: What CRMs have you used, and for how long on each?

Q3: What are your internet speed (download/upload) and your backup plan for power outages?

Q4: Can you commit to 9am–6pm Philippine Time, Monday–Friday, with occasional Saturday coverage? Do you currently have any other jobs or commitments during those hours?





Include your resume and a 60-second voice recording (Vocaroo or Google Drive link) answering: "Why should we hire you for this specific role?"



Applications missing any of the above will not be reviewed. We are strict about this because attention to detail is critical in this role.

title: Product Research Specialist (Ecommerce)
description:
Compensation: Paid per task



Let’s be direct.  

This role exists to find products that sell. Not ideas. Not trends for fun. Winners.



If you like guessing, this is not the job. If you like evidence, keep reading.



The Mission  

You identify products with real demand, clear positioning, and pricing that fits a structured store. Your work feeds the entire pipeline. Bad research poisons everything downstream.



What You Will Do  

• Research products for ecommerce stores  

• Validate demand using platform-native signals  

• Identify clear product angles and use cases  

• Analyze pricing logic and margin viability  

• Flag saturation, gimmicks, and weak offers  

• Organize findings cleanly for handoff to the team



Research Standards (Non-Negotiable)  

• Products must fit defined pricing tiers:

  – Low budget  

  – Mid quality  

  – Upscale  

• No random pricing logic  

• No novelty junk  

• No trend chasing without proof  

• Products must support clean branding and ad testing



What “Done” Looks Like  

Each task delivers:  

• 3–5 vetted product candidates  

• Clear reason each product should sell  

• Target price range and positioning tier  

• Notes on audience, pain point, and angle  

• Links and reference sources  

• Clean formatting. No clutter.



Pay Structure  

• Paid per completed research task  

• Consistent work if output is usable  

• Accuracy and judgment matter more than speed



Requirements  

• Proven product research experience  

• Strong pattern recognition  

• Understands ecommerce fundamentals and margins  

• Comfortable saying “this won’t work”  

• Clear written communication  

• Reliable availability



You’ll Do Well Here If  

• You eliminate bad products fast  

• You think in systems, not trends  

• You understand that most products fail  

• You don’t get emotionally attached to ideas



How to Apply  

Send:  

1. A brief summary of your product research experience  

2. One example of a product you would reject and why  

3. Your availability



All products will go through management.

title: Performance Video Editor / Media Buyer
description:
Compensation: Paid per task



Let’s get aligned fast.  

This role exists to make ads convert. Not to impress. Not to experiment. To sell.



If you are optimizing for aesthetics, this is not the job. If you are optimizing for response, keep reading.



The Mission  

You edit short-form ads designed to stop the scroll and trigger action. Every cut has a purpose. Every second earns its place.



Platforms: TikTok, Instagram Reels,
.



What You Will Do  

• Edit short-form paid ads (8–12 seconds)  

• Build a strong hook in the first 2 seconds or the ad dies  

• Control pacing, cuts, captions, and rhythm for retention  

• Follow brand references exactly. No interpretation.  

• Produce multiple variations per task for testing



What “Done” Looks Like  

Each task requires:  

• 3–6 ad variations  

• Clean exports, correctly named  

• Hook-driven structure:

  – Pattern interrupt  

  – Immediate product clarity  

  – Emotional trigger  

  – Clear call to action  

• Delivery within the agreed deadline. No drift.



Pay Structure  

• Task-based pay  

• Typical weekly range: $40–$50  

• Volume and performance determine upside



Requirements  

• Proven experience editing paid ads (portfolio required)  

• Understands conversion, not just visuals  

• Proficient in CapCut or Premiere Pro  

• Can follow references with precision  

• Fast, reliable, detail-focused



You’ll Win Here If  

• You think in hooks, not transitions  

• You can kill your favorite edit if it hurts performance  

• You deliver clean work without reminders  

• You understand that ads are systems, not art projects



How to Apply  

Send:  

1. Your paid ads portfolio (not cinematic edits)  

2. The platform you edit on  

3. Your typical turnaround time per task



This role is twice as important because it handles 2 things that come together for the most important piece: conversion.

title: Brand & Content Strategist — Men's Fashion Brand (Part-Time, Remote)
description:
We are MECENAS, a Portuguese men's fashion brand built around an aesthetic we call "Old Money Rebelde" — Elegant Lisbon, Atlantic coast, classic European style, adventure. Think Peaky Blinders meets the Portuguese coastline. We are looking for a Brand & Content Strategist who will plan every piece of content we shoot, direct the person executing on the ground in Portugal, and build the Instagram presence that becomes the engine of this brand.

This is a remote, part-time role (20–25 hours per week). You will not touch a camera. You will think, research, direct, and write briefs.



What you will actually do, week by week:



Study what is working right now on Instagram for brands in our world — menswear, old money, classic European style, cinematic lifestyle content. Identify trends we should replicate.

Build detailed shot lists and content briefs for our on-the-ground photographer/videographer in Lisbon. Every brief must be so clear that they can execute without asking a single question.

Plan our Instagram grid and Reels calendar. D
 what gets posted, when, and why.

Source visual references — moodboards, reference images, competitor breakdowns — for every shoot.

Write captions in English (Portuguese is a plus but not required).

Review content coming back from Portugal, give feedback, iterate.

Spot viral formats from other brands and adapt them to MECENAS aesthetic.





Who we are looking for:



You have 2+ years working with fashion, lifestyle, or premium brands — either in-house, at an agency, or as a freelancer running client accounts.

You have a trained eye for Western fashion and old money aesthetics. You can instantly tell the difference between "old money" and "flashy luxury." You know why a vintage Porsche is on-brand for us and a new Range Rover is not.

You are obsessed with Instagram. You know what is performing this month, not last year.

You can write a shot list so detailed that someone halfway across the world can execute it without messaging you back.

Your English is strong in writing. Spoken English is a plus but not critical.

You are self-directed. We will not micromanage you. You will own the content strategy and move it forward weekly.





References to calibrate your taste against:

Our brand world lives at the intersection of these references. If these names mean something to you, keep reading. If they do not, this role is probably not for you.



Brands: Parijan, Jay Howwit, Lohause, Art of Mondays

Movie Characters: Bruce Wayne, The Godfather, Tommy Shelby, James Bond


 @marvinbrooks

Aesthetic anchors: vintage Porsche or Land Rover, Omega Seamaster, Alfama at blue hour, surf at dawn in Cascais





The details:



Hours: 20–25 per week, flexible schedule. You set your own hours as long as briefs are delivered on time.

Compensation: $500–$700 USD per month, depending on experience and portfolio. Paid monthly.

Start: We want to start within 2 weeks of finding the right person.

Trial: First 4 weeks are a paid trial. If both sides are happy, we move into ongoing engagement.

Growth path: If this works, the role expands. We plan to hire a second executor in Portugal that you will also direct. Strong performers grow with the brand.





How to apply (read this part carefully):

Applications that do not follow these instructions are deleted without being read. We are serious about this filter.

Send us an
/message containing all five of the following:



The word "ATLANTIC" at the top of your message. This confirms you read the post fully.

Three examples of content strategy or Instagram accounts you have worked on. Send links. If your work is under NDA, describe the brand, the role you played, and the results. We want to see fashion, lifestyle, or premium positioning — not beauty salons or local restaurants.

Answer this in 3–5 sentences: What is the single biggest difference between "old money" aesthetic and "luxury flex" aesthetic, in visual content terms? There is no trick answer. We want to see how you think.

Pick one Reel or Instagram post from any menswear brand (not MECENAS) that you think is excellent. Send the link. In 3 sentences, tell us why it works and what you would steal from it for a brand like ours.

Tell us your monthly rate for 20–25 hours per week, and confirm your time zone.



Applications without the word ATLANTIC, without links, or without the short answers will not be considered.

We read every application that follows the instructions. Shortlisted candidates will get a paid mini-project (one shot-list brief, 2 hours of work, $30 flat) before we d
.

Looking forward to reading your work.

— The MECENAS team

title: Content & Production Specialist for a Pet Company (Full-time/Long-term/Remote)
description:
We’re hiring a Content & Production Specialist to support our Content, Editorial, & Social Systems team across posting, inbox management, and day-to-day operations.



In this role, you’ll be responsible for executing structured, repeatable tasks while supporting Content Managers with updates, live article edit coordination, and general content and production workflows. Your domain will be keeping content accurate, organized, and moving forward, while knowing when to take initiative and when to ask questions.



About us:



Pangolia is a fully remote, international company. We run high-traffic content and media websites that reach millions of readers each month, with a focus on the pet world.



About the Role:



You’ll be supporting multiple tea
mbers across content posting, live post edit workflows, inbox management, and operational tasks. Much of your work will follow clear processes — but not everything will.



This role requires someone who can balance accuracy, initiative, and communication. You should feel confident taking action within guidelines, but equally comfortable pausing to clarify when something doesn’t fit the usual process.



This is not a passive, task-only role. We’re looking for someone thoughtful, reliable, and communicative.



What You’ll Be Doing:



- Post and update content based on established guidelines

- Assist with live post edit workflows (applying flagged updates, formatting, and supporting content revisions)

- Help manage shared inboxes (sorting, flagging, and responding where appropriate)

- Follow structured processes for recurring tasks with high accuracy

- Support Content Managers with ad hoc updates and requests



What Success Looks Like:



- Content updates and posts are accurate and consistent

- Live edits are applied carefully and correctly

- Inbox communication is clear, timely, and organized

- Tasks are completed reliably with minimal to no errors

- Questions are raised at the right time — not too late, not unnecessarily early



Musts for the role:



- Available 40 hours a week 

- Strong written and spoken English

- Highly organized and detail-oriented  - In the first line of your application, please let us know what your favorite animal is (we are a pet company, after all!)

- Comfortable with repetitive tasks, but able to think critically when needed

- Experience with Google Sheets, Google Docs, WordPress

- Knows when to take initiative and when to ask for guidance

- Communicates clearly and proactively

- Experience in content, admin, or virtual assistant-style roles

- Reliable internet



Bonus (Nice to Have):

- Basic video editing (short-form content, reels, simple cuts)

- Experience supporting content updates or edits

- Podcast production or promotion

- Outreach experience for interviews/guest articles

What You Can Expect From Us:

Perks:

- Internet reimbursement



To apply, please include:



- A brief introduction of yourself

- Relevant experience - have you worked in this area before?

- Why does this role interest you?

- Anything else you think would help us understand how you'd thrive in this position



Can't wait to hear from you! 



Nicole Cosgrove

Director of Content, Editorial, & Social Systems

title: Outbound Calling Virtual Assistant – Appointment Setter (Recruiting + Client Calls)
description:
**Job Overview:**

We are hiring a **high-performance outbound calling Virtual Assistant** to help grow our insurance agency.



This role has two main responsibilities:



1. **Recruiting agents** (PRIMARY) – calling leads and setting them for a **Career Overview**

2. **Client appointment setting** (SECONDARY) – calling seniors who requested life insurance information



This is a **high-volume outbound calling role**. You must be confident on the phone and comfortable handling objections.



**Key Responsibilities:**



* Make **80–150 outbound calls per day**

* Call **agent leads (licensed & non-licensed)** and schedule them for a **Career Overview call**

* Clearly and briefly explain the opportunity and create interest

* Call **client leads (seniors)** and set appointments for licensed agents

* Send SMS follow-ups and confirmations

* Reduce no-shows by confirming appointments

* Update CRM with accurate notes and call outcomes



**What You Will Be Setting:**



* **Agents ? Career Overview appointments**



  * Licensed agents (ready to produce)

  * Non-licensed individuals (interested in getting licensed)



* **Clients ? Appointment with licensed agent**



**Requirements:**



* **At least 1 year of outbound calling or appointment setting experience (REQUIRED)**

* Comfortable making **80–150 calls per day**

* Clear, confident English speaking voice

* Ability to handle objections and keep calls efficient

* Comfortable speaking with both **agents and senior clients**

* Reliable internet + quiet work environment

* Available to work **U.S. EST hours**



**Compensation:**



* **$500/month base salary**

* **$2 per appointment set**

* **$4 per appointment that shows**



**Performance Bonus:**



* Additional bonus for high-quality candidates (agents who show strong interest or move forward)



**Expected Earnings:**



* Most VAs earn **$800 – $1,400+ per month** based on performance





**Preferred Experience (Bonus):**



* Insurance or final expense industry
